title: MSA & Order Form amendment — paperwork stitch and next steps
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 1 Mar 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MSA & Order Form amendment — stitch plan

Team —

Clearinghouse call with Clearwater went well this morning. High level: they want to extend term from 12→24 months, add a 30% capacity uplift on our NPU pool, and surface one data-residency clause for EU workloads (they said they will sign a data-scope addendum later). They sent a tracked-change MSA and a revised order form (attached) with a handful of commercial asks.

Immediate asks:
- Markus: can you run through the attached tracked MSA and flag any commercial/legal risk items — my priorities are liability cap, termination for convenience, and data-residency placeholders.
- Mateo: can you own the order form alignment and confirm the capacity tiers we can commit to for Q2/Q3?
- Grace: please prepare a one-pager for account (Clearwater) that summarizes the billing cadence and uplift impact for customer success.

Goal: get a consolidated internal position and one consolidated customer response by EOD Monday (2029-03-04).

Attachments: Clearwater_MSA_2029_03_track_changes.docx (track changes), OrderForm_AMEND_V2.xlsx

Thanks,

Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Sr. Renewals Manager | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com


From: Samantha Park <samantha.park@clearwater.io>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Clearwater Contracts <contracts@clearwater.io>
Date: Sat, 2 Mar 2029 10:05:21 -0500
Subject: Re: MSA & Order Form amendment — stitch plan

Hi Lauren / Mateo —

Thanks for the call. Attaching the MSA with our edits and the updated order form that reflects the 24-month term and the proposed 30% uplift. A few clarifications inline:

- Term: we moved to a 24-month auto-renew with a 90-day notice to opt-out.
- Pricing: uplift line items included in sheet "Tiered Uplift"; we kept the per-token floor but added a usage band for burst.
- Compliance: we added a paragraph to require redwood to provide audit logs on request (we expect this to be a standard ask).
- Data residency: placeholder indicating EU data will have separate SOW/addendum.

We are aiming to finalize legal language and commercial terms within two weeks so our finance team can commit headcount to the integration schedule.

Attachments: Clearwater_MSA_2029_03_track_changes.docx (customer tracked), OrderForm_AMEND_V2.xlsx

Best,
Samantha Park
Head of Procurement | Clearwater Analytics
samantha.park@clearwater.io


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Mar 2029 08:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA & Order Form amendment — stitch plan

Thanks Samantha (and Lauren) — copying internal next steps to keep momentum.

Summary of internal ask and proposed cadence:
1) Commercial sync (today EOD PDT): Lauren and I to reconcile order form numbers; I will confirm which capacity tiers we can guarantee for the 24-month term and the headroom we can offer for burst.
2) Legal narrow pass (Markus): take the customer tracked MSA and provide a short comment doc outlining must-hold positions and acceptable concessions. Focus items: liability cap alignment to TCV, termination for convenience language, and a scoped audit-log response SLA.
3) Account messaging (Grace): 1-page summary for customer success and finance that shows the monthly invoice impact, any proration on term change, and the uplift commitment schedule.
4) Consolidated reply to customer by Tue 2029-03-05 COB (their two-week target). Use a single attachment bundle: our annotated MSA (clean comments, not tracked), and a reconciled OrderForm_V2.xlsx.

Risk notes for Markus: if the audit-log ask includes continuous access or export of infra-level logs, escalate to Security; we should offer a scoped extracts cadence (quarterly or on-demand with defined scope) rather than full exports. Also flag data-residency language as "placeholder" — we will accept an addendum route but do not commit to hosting changes without a separate SOW.

Action owners: Lauren (commercial reconcile), Markus (legal pass), Grace (one-pager). I'll draft the draft customer reply and upload to the Drive link below by EOD today for review.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aZ-clarify-contracts-CLW-2029
DocuSign preview (orderform): https://app.docusign.com/CLW-2029/orderform-sign

Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Principal Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Mar 2029 15:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA & Order Form amendment — stitch plan

Mateo / Lauren —

Quick legal pass attached (summary comments inline). Key positions:
- Liability cap: propose cap = 12 months of fees (we can stretch to 18 months only with explicit underwriting from Sales Ops).
- Termination for convenience: accept a 90-day notice but add a pro-rata payment for the remainder of any pre-purchased capacity that cannot be reallocated within 60 days.
- Audit logs: offer on-demand extracts with an SLA (10 business days) and specify the format and redaction policy. Do not agree to continuous streaming or infra-level exports.
- EU data/residency: accept placeholder language and require a formal SOW for any hosting changes; include a non-binding timeline to open discussions.

I recommend we provide the customer a clean MSA with our redlines shown as comments (not tracked changes) and a reconciled order form that calls out the uplift bands. If Sales wants the 18-month liability cap, we need a written risk acceptance from Finance/Leadership.

Attaching: MK_legal_pass_Clearwater_MSA_comments.docx

Markus Klein
Head of Contracts | Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwood.com

title: Capacity surcharge clarification & order-form sign path
preview:
From: Elena Park <elena.park@solstice.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Oct 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Capacity uplift & a new 'surcharge' line in our PO

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday — the team is excited to increase usage. Procurement routed a draft PO (attached) that includes an added 'capacity surcharge' line our internal billing team wants to confirm with you. High level: we plan to uplift committed capacity by ~40% starting Nov 1, and procurement's legal added a clause that describes a monthly \"capacity surcharge\" for peaks above committed baseline.

Before we escalate to Solstice legal, can you confirm whether Redwood has a standard approach here (overage pricing vs. an explicit surcharge), and whether that impacts term length? We're also trying to align on term: our C-suite prefers 18 months if we can secure the current unit price and some on-boarding credits.

Attached: Solstice_PO_987.pdf

Would appreciate a quick call if helpful.

Best,
Elena Park
Head of Platform, Solstice AI
elena.park@solstice.ai
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0123


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@solstice.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Oct 2028 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Capacity uplift & a new 'surcharge' line in our PO

Hi Elena — thanks for the note and the PO. Congrats again on the expansion plans.

Quick answers up front: Redwood typically treats those as overage billing (per-unit overage price) rather than an undefined \"surcharge\" line. We can accommodate the 18-month term at the current unit price, subject to a short SLA credit arrangement and legal review on the wording.

Looping in Karthik (commercial) and Lauren (legal) to propose exact redline language and a clean order form we can push to DocuSign. Priyom — FYI for billing sequencing and any temporary credit we can apply during onboarding.

I'll prepare a clean order-form draft and a proposed redline for the MSA that replaces 'surcharge' with a clear overage rate + monthly cap.

Attaching: Redwood_OrderForm_Draft_v1.pdf (draft)

Would any of you be available for a 20-min sync Monday morning PST?

Thanks,
Ben
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-3478


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Oct 2028 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed redline language for 'surcharge' -> overage pricing

Team — quick pass on the legal/commercial framing before we send to Solstice counsel. Key points:

- Replace vague \"capacity surcharge\" term with an \"Overage Unit Price\" defined as the per-unit price applied to token usage beyond the committed baseline for the billing month.
- Add a monthly cap equal to 150% of the committed baseline spend to limit variability.
- For term, accept 18 months at current unit price but add a 3% annual escalator after month 12 (to align with cost trajectory). Alternatively we can offer flat price for entire 18 months if they commit to a minimum ARR uplift (we need to confirm target).
- Offer: 2 weeks of onboarding credits (applied as token credits) and a one-time 30% discount on the first month's overage for peace-of-mind.

I drafted suggested replaces and have it as an attached redline. Lauren / legal: can you confirm whether the cap wording is acceptable or if we should instead include a 'not-to-exceed' yearly liability cap?

Attachment: msaredline_karthik_v1.docx

Karthik Iyer
Head of Commercial Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Elena Park <elena.park@solstice.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@solsticelegal.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Oct 2028 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Capacity uplift & a new 'surcharge' line in our PO

Thanks all — appreciate the quick turnaround. I'm copying our counsel, Olivia. A few clarifications from procurement/corp dev:

- The 150% monthly cap sounds reasonable.
- Solstice will accept an overage unit price but wants an explicit monthly notification requirement when projected usage will hit 90% of committed baseline (so we can plan budget and throttles).
- Regarding term: Solstice accepts 18 months at the current unit price, but our CFO requested a performance credit if latency P50 exceeds 200ms for two consecutive weeks in a month (we'd prefer a discount applied to that month's invoice).

If you can send a clean order form + tracked redline for the MSA with the proposed changes and the notification commitment, we'll get it to Olivia and return an annotated counter quickly.

Thanks,
Elena
Head of Platform, Solstice AI

On Sat, Oct 20, 2028 at 10:04 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — quick pass on the legal/commercial framing before we send to Solstice counsel. Key points:
>
> - Replace vague \"capacity surcharge\" term with an \"Overage Unit Price\" defined as the per-unit price applied to token usage beyond the committed baseline for the billing month.
> - Add a monthly cap equal to 150% of the committed baseline spend to limit variability.
> - For term, accept 18 months at current unit price but add a 3% annual escalator after month 12 (to align with cost trajectory). Alternatively we can offer flat price for entire 18 months if they commit to a minimum ARR uplift (we need to confirm target).
> - Offer: 2 weeks of onboarding credits (applied as token credits) and a one-time 30% discount on the first month's overage for peace-of-mind.
>
> Attachment: msaredline_karthik_v1.docx


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@solsticelegal.com>, Elena Park <elena.park@solstice.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Oct 2028 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood redline & DocuSign path (MSA + Order Form)

Olivia / Elena — thanks for the clarifications. Attaching a tracked redline of the MSA (MSA_Redline_Lauren_v2.docx) that:

- Replaces \"capacity surcharge\" with a defined \"Overage Unit Price\" and a clear notification requirement when usage is projected to reach 90% of baseline.
- Inserts a monthly cap set at 150% of committed baseline spend.
- Declines an explicit monthly discount-based performance credit tied to P50 latency (legal will not carve out SLA credits that way), but offers a commercially equivalent remediation: a one-time invoice credit up to 2 weeks of average monthly spend if Redwood fails to meet published latency SLA for two consecutive weeks.
- Confirms we can accommodate an 18-month term at current unit price and proposes a one-time onboarding credit (2 weeks of token credits) plus a DocuSign-ready order form.

I've also opened a DocuSign envelope for the order form to streamline signature once counsel signs off. Link is below. If Solstice requires alternative language, please propose counters in the redline and we'll iterate quickly.

Attachments:
- MSA_Redline_Lauren_v2.docx
- Redwood_OrderForm_Draft_v1.pdf (clean order form)

DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/link/ABC123-redwood-solstice

Best,
Lauren Bishop
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
lauren_bishop@redwood.com


From: Elena Park <elena.park@solstice.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@solsticelegal.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Oct 2028 16:50:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood redline & DocuSign path (MSA + Order Form)

Lauren — Olivia and I reviewed the redline. We're good with the overage wording and the 150% cap; the notification clause is acceptable as written. We appreciate the onboarding credits and the SLA remediation credit alternative.

Signed the order form via DocuSign and attached the signed file for your records. Procurement will issue the formal PO that references DEAL-8842 in the coming business day.

Attachment: signed_orderform_solstice.pdf

Thanks everyone for the quick turn. Looking forward to go-live first week of Nov.

Best,
Elena
Head of Platform, Solstice AI

> - Confirms we can accommodate an 18-month term at current unit price and proposes a one-time onboarding credit (2 weeks of token credits) plus a DocuSign-ready order form.

title: BridgePoint DPA redline and SCCs: mapping & execution cadence
preview:
From: Emma Li <emma.li@bridgepointhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Nora Byrne <nora.byrne@bridgepoint-legal.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Sep 2028 13:22:00 +0100
Subject: BridgePoint - DPA redline (initial)

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the call this morning. Attached is our legal team's initial redline to your standard DPA and the SCCs annex we usually push for when we receive processor services that may route data outside the UK/EU. Please note the key asks:

- Clarify controller/processor mapping across the Order Form and DPA (we flagged section 3).
- Add an export/transfer-impact addendum for third-country subprocessors.
- KMS and key separation: we require evidence of customer-managed keys or clear mitigations.
- Retention and deletion confirmations for project data on Dedicated and Private deployments.

We've also highlighted a handful of liability caps and audit-window timing we'd like to discuss. For now, can you confirm who on your side will own the DPA review and whether Redwood can provide the SCCs Annex in editable format so we can align faster?

Attachments: bridgepoint_dpa_redline_v1.docx; sccs-annex-mapping.xlsx

Best,
Emma Li
Head of Legal Operations
BridgePoint Health
emma.li@bridgepointhealth.com
+44 20 7000 1234


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Li <emma.li@bridgepointhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 13 Sep 2028 09:04:00 +0100
Subject: Re: BridgePoint - DPA redline (initial)

Hi Emma — thanks for sending this through and for the clear flags. I've looped Legal (Laura) and Privacy (Irene) here for the technical and legal specifics, and Priyom for the order form/ commercial impact.

Quick answers:
- Laura will lead legal redline coordination from our side.
- We can provide the SCCs annex in editable Word + Excel (sharing link shortly).
- KMS: our Private deployment supports customer-managed keys via KMS/HSM; for Dedicated we can document key separation controls — I'll confirm evidence artifacts.

Next steps proposed:
1) Laura to run a quick read and indicate high level accept/exception list by EOD Thu (14th).
2) Technical follow-up (Irene) to confirm data residency and KMS evidence by Mon (18th).
3) Commercial (Priyom) to map any pricing/SLA items tied to addendum changes.

If that cadence works for you we'll set a short sync for Tue (19th) to close the major items.

Mateo
Sr. Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Li <emma.li@bridgepointhealth.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 14 Sep 2028 15:18:00 +0100
Subject: Re: BridgePoint - DPA redline (initial)

Emma / Mateo — I've reviewed the redline and attached a short comment set. High-level observations:

1) Roles & mapping: BridgePoint's proposed language pushes more controller obligations into the processor (our side). We need to preserve the existing controller/processor split in the MSA/Order Form; redlines that shift operational obligations to Redwood will require scope and pricing changes.

2) SCCs annex: acceptable in principle. We will provide editable versions and propose a reconciliation table mapping clause-by-clause to our standard Annex to make any divergences explicit.

3) Transfer-impact addendum: we can accept a targeted addendum for subprocessor routing, but we need clarity about origin countries and any onshore-only hosting requirements. If you require strict EU-only hosting for certain datasets, that will drive Dedicated/Private scope.

4) Audit & evidence: BridgePoint's request for a 30-day audit-window is reasonable; we'll propose a standard process (advance notice, scoped) and require a cost-recovery fee for extensive on-site audits.

Ben, can you estimate what the KMS evidence bundle looks like (logs, key rotation policy, HSM attestations)? Irene — please propose a mapping table for controller/processor responsibilities we can circulate.

I'll compile our comment set as a redline and circulate by Fri morning.

—Laura
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Privacy
Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Emma Li <emma.li@bridgepointhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 16 Sep 2028 10:05:00 +0100
Subject: Re: BridgePoint - DPA redline (initial)

Laura — initial KMS evidence summary below (I'll package formal artifacts to the Drive folder):

- Key management options: customer-managed keys via AWS KMS/HSM (for Private / VPC deployments) OR Redwood-managed keys with tenant isolation for Hosted/Dedicated.
- Evidence bundle (to follow): key policy export, rotation schedule, last 12 months rotation log, HSM attestation (where applicable), encryption-in-transit certs, and a diagram of key separation boundaries.
- Subprocessor key access: our CSPs do not have access to plaintext keys when customer-managed keys are in use. For Redwood-managed keys, we maintain split-key management and strict role-based access with audit trails.

Notes for commercial: customer-managed keys for Dedicated/Private are supported but may require a short setup fee and runbook.

Ben
Security & Compliance Lead
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>

Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 18 Sep 2028 16:10:00 +0100
Subject: Re: BridgePoint - DPA redline (initial)

If BridgePoint needs hard EU-only guarantees for project PII, we'll need to move to Dedicated EU or Private (on-prem/VPC) for that scope; that change triggers an order form amendment and a one-time onboarding / capacity reservation fee.

2) Execution steps:
- Tue 19 Sep: 30 minute sync (legal + technical + commercial) to agree mapping and finalize which datasets require EU-only handling.
- Wed 20 Sep: Laura to provide Redwood redline vs BridgePoint redline (tracked) and reconciliation table.
- Thu 21 Sep: Exchange final DPA + Order Form amendments and open DocuSign envelope for signatures.

3) DocuSign & artifacts: I uploaded a draft order form (orderform-draft-BP-042.pdf) and a pre-filled DocuSign envelope. Link: https://docusign.net/Envelope/ABC-1234

Emma — can you confirm availability for Tue 19 Sep 10:00 CET? If yes we will lock the calendar and share an agenda.

Thanks,
Priyom Das

Redwood Inference

title: Two-part QBR proposal: async pre-read + 30m exec sync
preview:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Clara Zhang <clara@shiftly.com>, Eli Torres <eli.torres@shiftly.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Mar 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Two-part QBR proposal: async pre-read + 30m exec sync

Hi Clara / Eli —

As we approach the renewal QBR for Shiftly (thanks for the great partnership last year), I wanted to propose a slightly different format that will make it easier for your execs while still giving us a solid roadmap alignment. Proposal:

1) Asynchronous pre-read (45–60 minutes of materials + 10–15 min recorded walkthrough) delivered 48 hours before the live meeting.
2) 30-minute live executive sync for decisions, top questions and next steps.

Benefits: keeps the live meeting concise for busy execs, lets practitioners get into technical clarifications ahead of time, and reduces travel/timezone pressure for APAC stakeholders.

Attached: a lightweight QBR agenda and a one-page prework checklist. If this works, we can offer a few 30-min exec slots across PST/EST next week. Below are options — please flag preferences and any stakeholders you want on the live session.

Proposed 30-min live windows (pick 2):
- Tue Mar 28: 10:00–10:30 PT / 1:00–1:30 ET
- Wed Mar 29: 13:00–13:30 PT / 4:00–4:30 ET
- Thu Mar 30: 08:00–08:30 PT / 11:00–11:30 ET

Ops note: if you prefer a single 60-min workshop instead, we can accommodate, but recommend the two-part model for clarity.

Thanks — happy to iterate.

Best,
Jonas
Head of Customer Success (Renewals)
Redwood Inference
[attachment: QBR-Agenda-v1.pdf (pdf, 190KB)]
[attachment: prework-asks.md (markdown, 4KB)]

Cc: Eli Torres <eli.torres@shiftly.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Mar 2028 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Two-part QBR proposal: async pre-read + 30m exec sync

Thanks Jonas — this sounds reasonable and matches how our execs prefer to consume material. A couple quick notes:

- We like the async pre-read idea (we'll distribute to product and analytics teams).
- Exec sync attendees: myself, CTO (Eli for now), and potentially our VP of Ops (TBD).
- Best live window for us is Wed Mar 29 13:00 PT (4:00 ET). If necessary, Thu morning is a fallback.

Can you confirm what pre-reads you need from our side? Also note we have a governance meeting on Apr 2 — if there are any decision items that need a formal sign-off, we want to make sure they hit that meeting.

Appreciate it,
Clara
Director, Platform Strategy
Shiftly

- If you'd like a short demo clip of the new batching/caching dashboard, indicate who should receive the clip (platform/ops/prod).

Ops attachments: I've attached a runbook and a sample prework template. We'll also provision a short recording link and a synced Drive folder.

Soojin

title: Log egress options and purge guarantees for EU tenant — FinServCorp
preview:
From: Andrew Miller <andrew.miller@finservcorp.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Ops <legal-ops@finservcorp.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Feb 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Log egress and purge guarantees for our EU tenant

Hi Mateo,

We're preparing for an audit and our legal team needs clear answers on two points for our EU tenant:

1) Can Redwood export both access logs and audit logs on demand in a tenant-scoped, per-tenant NDJSON or CSV format? If so, what destinations are supported (S3/GCS/Azure Blob, SFTP)?

2) Once Redwood deletes tenant data (for retention or at our request), can you provide a signed attestation and a manifest of the exported records? Also need to confirm: what exact retention windows apply to audit vs access logs in EU (including any cold backups or snapshots you maintain), and how long they are kept before being wiped from backups?

We expect PHI-adjacent metadata in some logs — legal wants to be sure you do not retain any customer-provided PII in long-term backups beyond the retention window.

Attached a short list of evidence items we're expecting as part of the audit (audit-export-manifest.csv).

Thanks — appreciate a rundown and any playbook / export runbook you can share.

Andrew
Andrew Miller
Head of IT Compliance
FinServCorp

[Attachment: audit-export-manifest.csv (CSV)]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Andrew Miller <andrew.miller@finservcorp.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Feb 2028 08:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Log egress and purge guarantees for our EU tenant

Hi Andrew — thanks for the clear list. Sharing a concise summary up-front, then looping in Rishi and Marissa (infra + product) for details and runbook links:

Summary:
- Export formats: tenant-scoped exports available in NDJSON or CSV. Supported destinations: Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, Azure Blob Storage, and secure SFTP endpoints. Exports are encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest using customer-managed keys if requested.
- Retention: default audit-log retention (hosted) is 90 days; access logs (request-level traces) default 30 days. For Enterprise (contracted) tenants we offer customizable retention up to 2 years for audit logs and 1 year for access logs via Dedicated/Private plans.
- Backups & snapshots: snapshots for disaster recovery are retained for a rolling 14-day window in EU-region-only deployments. We do not keep long-term backups of tenant log content beyond configured retention unless under legal hold.
- Deletion & attestation: we provide an export manifest and deletion attestation for tenant-initiated erasure. For legal holds, we mark records and suspend scheduled deletion until hold removal.

I'll let Rishi confirm the technical export cadence and the exact fields we include by default (for PII minimization), and Marissa will add the compliance playbook link and SLA references.

Next steps: Rishi — can you confirm the default field set, whether we persist raw request bodies in audit logs for EU tenants, and the exact snapshots/backups policy? Marissa — please attach the export playbook and SLA table.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

> On Sat, Feb 18, 2028 at 9:12 AM Andrew Miller <andrew.miller@finservcorp.com> wrote:
> > We're preparing for an audit and our legal team needs clear answers on two points for our EU tenant: ...
> > [Attachment: audit-export-manifest.csv (CSV)]


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Andrew Miller <andrew.miller@finservcorp.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Feb 2028 10:15:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Log egress and purge guarantees for our EU tenant

Hi all — technical clarifications below. Keeping it focused so Andrew's legal team can map items to their checklist.

1) Export payload / fields:
- Audit logs: include timestamp, tenant_id, actor_id (redacted if requested), operation, resource_path, result_code, request_id, server_region. We exclude request/response bodies from audit logs by default in EU-hosted tenants. That minimizes PHI/PII surface.
- Access logs (request traces): include timestamp, tenant_id, route, latency_ms, node_id, and a hashed client_ip (salted, reversible on request for sanctioned investigations). We do not persist plaintext client IPs in long-term exports unless customer explicitly requests them and signs a Data Processing Amendment.

2) Export mechanisms:
- Ad-hoc exports: initiated via Console (Export -> Tenant snapshot) or API. Exports generate a signed manifest and are pushed to the target destination you select.
- Scheduled exports: available (daily/weekly/monthly) for Enterprise customers. Exports are chunked into time-bounded NDJSON files (max 4GB per chunk).
- Format: NDJSON preferred for streaming/ingest; CSV available for smaller slices.

3) Retention & backups:
- Hosted EU tenants: audit logs default 90 days, access logs 30 days.
- Enterprise Dedicated/Private: configurable up to audit=24 months, access=12 months via contract.
- Backups/snapshots: short-lived DR snapshots (14 days) in-region; cold backups are not used for tenant-level logs. We do not export tenant logs to internal long-term archival systems.

4) Deletion flow & attestation:
- Standard deletion: once retention lapses or customer requests erasure, records are removed from primary store and removed from snapshot caches during next snapshot rotation.
- Attestation: we produce a signed deletion statement (PDF) and include an export manifest listing file checksums and object keys as evidence.
- Legal holds: process: Legal Ops attaches a hold; we suspend deletions, mark records in the index; exportable as-is. Removing a hold requires documented approval from Legal Ops.

5) Edge cases & compliance notes:
- If a customer requires that no reversible hashes exist for client IPs, we can salt-and-drop reversible fields and only keep non-reversible hashes.
- For cross-border concerns: EU-region hosted tenants keep all log content in EU by default. Exports to non-EU destinations require a customer-signed data transfer addendum.

Attachments: I'm attaching the internal export options doc and the SLA extract for export delivery SLAs.

Rishi Malhotra
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood-log-export-options.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: sla-export-table.pdf (PDF)]

> On Mon, Feb 20, 2028 at 8:23 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Summary: Export formats: tenant-scoped exports available in NDJSON or CSV...

title: Request: Narrow DPA runtime access scope for EdgePilot POC
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira.k@edgepilot.io>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: EdgePilot Legal <legal@edgepilot.io>
Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Narrow runtime access ask for POC (attached DPA redline)

Hi Kimberly —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. EdgePilot is excited to move the POC forward but our legal team flagged the runtime access language in your standard DPA. For this pilot we only need inference outputs and minimal telemetry for observability; we cannot permit runtime payload retention or index-level access to inference inputs.

Attached is a redline from our counsel that:
- restricts runtime access to aggregated telemetry only (no raw input storage),
- prohibits Redwood from using runtime inputs for any model training/retraining, and
- requests a list of subprocessors that touch runtime request data (if any) plus an allowlist/assurance.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept this narrowed scope for the 90-day POC? If not, please point to the minimal product/security exceptions needed.

Attachments: EdgePilot_DPA_redline_v3.pdf, runtime_scope_matrix.xlsx

Thanks — happy to set a quick call with legal if helpful.

Samira Khan
Head of Integrations | EdgePilot
samira.k@edgepilot.io
+1 415-555-0172


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@edgepilot.io>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2028 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Narrow runtime access ask for POC (attached DPA redline)

Samira —

Appreciate the clear asks and the redline. We're supportive of fast POC progress and want to make a path that preserves customer constraints while staying within our security policy. Quickly:

- Product: Mateo, can you confirm if we can deliver inference outputs with no raw request retention on our hosted POC path? Also need to confirm metrics/telemetry we can expose (aggregated counters vs. token-level timing).
- Security: Olga, please advise whether a temporary "runtime access exception" (90 days, limited to the pilot) with the controls Samira outlines is acceptable, and whether we need a signed attestation or extra controls (e.g., KMS scoping, no-training clause).
- Sales/ops: I'll coordinate procurement and DocuSign if we get alignment.

Proposed next steps:
1) Security to call out exact permitted telemetry fields and subprocessors list.
2) Product to confirm implementation plan and timeline (can we toggle retention per customer?).
3) If all green, Ben will send a short MPA + DocuSign link and we sign the pilot addendum.

Kimberly Park
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.com


From: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Samira Khan <samira.k@edgepilot.io>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jan 2028 10:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Narrow runtime access ask for POC (attached DPA redline)

Kimberly / Samira —

Thanks for the redline. Security has reviewed the attached and we can provision an exception for a time-boxed POC provided the following conditions are met:

- No raw request payloads are persisted to long-term storage. We can provide ephemeral in-memory inference handling and configured TTLs that expire in under 24 hours.
- Explicit "no training" language: Redwood will not use runtime inputs/outputs for model training, evaluation, or tuning for the duration of the POC (we will add a clause to the pilot addendum).
- Subprocessor transparency: We'll provide a named list of subprocessors that touch the request path (e.g., cloud infra, logging vendor). Any subprocessors that require additional assurances must be listed in the addendum; EdgePilot may request removal or additional contractual guarantees.
- Telemetry scope: We can expose aggregated metrics (requests/min, median latency, error rates) and anonymized sampling for debugging. Token-level traces or full-raw logs are not available under the exception.

Operational controls we will apply:
- Dedicated customer namespace for the POC with enforced retention policy.
- KMS scoping for any stored artifacts (if absolutely necessary, but we will avoid storing raw requests).
- Audit log export for access to runtime artifacts during the POC window.

If EdgePilot can accept those constraints, Security signs off and we can move to a short pilot addendum. I will attach an example addendum template and the subprocessors list separately this morning.

—Olga
Olga Petrov
Director, Product Security | Redwood Inference
olga@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@edgepilot.io>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Jan 2028 15:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Narrow runtime access ask for POC (attached DPA redline)

Samira —

Great — thanks all for the quick turnaround. Per Olga and Mateo's notes, plan to proceed as follows:

1) I will draft a one-page pilot addendum that inserts the no-training clause and enumerates the telemetry fields we agreed to (aggregated metrics, anonymized sampling).
2) Procurement: we'll route the addendum + the redline to DocuSign (DOCUSIGN://envelope/0a1b2c3d) and aim for signature within 3 business days.
3) Engineering: Mateo, please confirm ETA for toggling retention per customer and for providing the namespace. If there's any blocker, flag it by EOD Monday.

Samira — can you confirm EdgePilot Legal accepts the subprocessors list once I share it? If yes, I'll include the list as an appendix to the addendum.

Thanks — looking forward to getting the POC on the schedule.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0189

title: Re: EU catalog VAT & MSA signer orchestration — cross-border tax mapping
preview:
From: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Jean-Luc Martin <jl.martin@acme-eu.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2027 08:12:00 +0100
Subject: Catalog tax fields + MSA redline — EU VAT handling question

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the walkthrough earlier. We're trying to move the procurement portal activation forward but hit a snag on how Redwood will populate VAT handling on punchout/catelog orders that originate in our EU marketplaces. Two specific asks: 

1) Order Form: we need a clear mapping for VAT responsibilities (supplier-borne vs buyer-reverse-charge) per line item — currently our EDI and Ariba mappings expect a \"tax_indicator\" + \"vat_mode\" field. Can Redwood populate these from the catalog metadata or will we need an intermediary transform?

2) MSA redline: we attached our latest internal notes and the redline from Legal. There is a clause (Section 7.2) about cross-border tax indemnity that our tax team will not accept unless the Order Form explicitly records the VAT mode per PO line. Marissa, Mateo — looping in your legal/ops contacts here.

Attachments: Acme_MSA_Redline_v3.docx, OrderForm_EU_VAT_Spec.xlsx

Can your team propose a minimal change to the Order Form schema that keeps the MSA language intact but gives us the namespace for VAT mode? We are hoping to avoid an MSA amendment if possible — catalog metadata + orderform mapping would be ideal.

Best,

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>

Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2027 11:03:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Catalog tax fields + MSA redline — EU VAT handling question

Hi Sophie — thanks for the detail and the attachments. Quick notes from the ops side: 

- Redwood's standard Order Form includes an optional \"tax_mode\" attribute at the PO header level but not per-line by default. We do support per-line tax metadata in the catalog (we surface a \"vat_profile_id\" on catalog items that our runtime can carry through to PO line items).

- Short term option (no MSA amendment): we can add a per-line \"vat_mode\" passthrough on the connector that maps Acme's catalog \"VAT profile\" to the Order Form line attribute. That requires a connector mapping rule and a short onboarding task.

- Longer term: product would prefer adding \"vat_mode\" as a first-class optional Order Form line field (backwards compatible). That would require a small schema bump and rollout window (2–3 sprints).

Marissa / Mateo — Sophie highlighted Section 7.2 in their redline. Can legal confirm whether capturing \"vat_mode\" in the Order Form payload (and persisting it for audit) is acceptable from a contract perspective and avoids an MSA change? If you need the exact clause text, see attached redline.

Sophie — if you can share a sample PO XML snippet from Acme's Ariba side (we asked for the EDI sample, but an Ariba-XML example will be helpful), Mateo and I can produce a mapping spec and ETA.

Thanks,
Kimberly
Head of Deployments — Redwood Inference




From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Dec 2027 09:31:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Catalog tax fields + MSA redline — legal take and recommended path

Kim/Mateo/Sophie,

I reviewed Section 7.2 redline and spoke with tax counsel. Summary: 

- We prefer not to amend the MSA if we can capture unequivocal evidence on the transactional record that shows the billed VAT treatment per PO line. If the Order Form (or the incoming Ariba payload) carries an explicit \"vat_mode\" value and we retain that in audit logs for 7 years (per our retention policy for finance), that's an acceptable operational control that satisfies the indemnity concern.

- Two conditions from Legal: 
  1) The Order Form must include an enumerated set of accepted values (e.g., \"supplier_borne\", \"reverse_charge\", \"out_of_scope\"). Free-text is not acceptable.
  2) We must include a short term fallback policy: if the \"vat_mode\" is missing, Redwood will tag the line as \"unknown_vat\" and route the PO to a human review queue (we should document SLA for escalations).

Operational impact: Logan — ops will need to create the review queue and the connector mapping. Mateo — can you draft the mapping spec (catalog field -> orderform.vat_mode) and confirm how we will persist the field in the PO history? Sophie — if Acme can populate an enumerated value from your side, we'll be able to keep this as an onboarding task rather than an MSA amendment.

Attached: legal notes (inline comments on Acme_MSA_Redline_v3.docx).

Marissa
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference

2) Ops configuration (Logan) — will provision a human review queue and set an SLA: 8 business hours for tax-unknown flags during the onboarding phase. We'll also add monitoring alerts to the activation dashboard so your procurement team can see outstanding reviews.

3) Legal controls (Marissa) — Redwood will persist order.vat_mode in audit logs for at least 7 years and include the value in our PO-level export for finance. Marissa will provide a short clause we can include in the onboarding acceptance checklist (not an MSA amendment).

title: CloudCode webinar — promo pool & 90-day trial request
preview:
From: Susan Jennings <susan@devstreams.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Promo pool request for CloudCode webinar (Nov 30)

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for chatting earlier — excited to feature Redwood as our launch sponsor for the CloudCode Summit on Nov 30. We had a last-minute bump in registrants and the marketing team asked if Redwood can provide a bulk promo pool for attendees so we can offer a full 90-day trial (rather than the standard 30).

Details: 
- Headline ask: 300 trial activations, 90-day trial length, single-use codes per email
- Audience: mix of developers (60%), engineering managers (30%), and platform teams (10%)
- Use: distributed in post-webinar follow-up email + session resources page
- Required by: codes issued by Nov 22 so ops can embed them in the follow-up flow

Attached is a short brief with attendee breakdown and expected redemption cadence. Can you confirm feasibility and anything you need from our side?

Appreciate it — Susan

--
Susan Jennings
Partnerships Lead, DevStreams Events
susan@devstreams.com
(attachment: promo_request_brief.pdf)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Susan Jennings <susan@devstreams.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Nov 2027 11:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Promo pool request for CloudCode webinar (Nov 30)

Hi Susan — thanks for the brief and the attendee breakdown. Happy to help and congrats on the lift in registrations. 

Quick answers up front:
- We can provision a batch of single-use promo codes that map to 90-day trial accounts.
- Need final attendee list format (email column) and confirmation that codes will only be sent to registered emails.
- Ops needs an approvals handoff and a signed promo T&C (short form) — I attached a template we use.

Operational ask: Logan (cc'd) — can you confirm whether we have capacity to issue 300 codes with 90-day trial length and set the expiration window to 2028-01-31? Also flag any accounting limitations for promotional extensions of that duration.

Susan — if you can drop the attendee CSV by EOD Nov 18 we should be able to get codes to you by Nov 22.

Best,
Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com
(attachment: promo_terms_template.docx)

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Nov 2027 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Promo pool request for CloudCode webinar (Nov 30)

Marissa — booking this on ops calendar. Quick internal notes / checklist before we issue: 

1) Quota & backend: we can generate 300 single-use codes; the provisioning pipeline supports 90-day default trial length but it requires a promo flag so that accounting codes map to 'Event - Partner Promo Q4'.
2) Accounting: Mateo — need your sign-off on promo expense classification and whether we should cap per-company redemption to avoid abuse.
3) Legal / T&C: promo_terms_template.docx looks fine but legal prefers a clause that codes are non-transferable. If marketing wants to add an expiration on redemption, we should put that in.
4) Delivery: I will produce the CSV of codes and upload to the secure share with write-only access. Expect the file name: promo_codes_CloudCode_300.csv.

If Mateo and Marissa OK, I'll lock code parameters and schedule the issuance for Tue Nov 22 09:00 PT.

Thanks,
Logan

--
Logan Wright
Program Ops, Redwood Inference
logan@redwood.com (attachment: none)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Susan Jennings <susan@devstreams.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Nov 2027 17:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Promo pool request for CloudCode webinar (Nov 30)

All — confirmed and approved. Accounting reviewed and we'll classify as 'Marketing partnership promo Q4'. A few final conditions we asked Susan to accept (pasted below) — Susan, please acknowledge and we'll proceed to issuance on Monday (Nov 22) morning.

Conditions: 
- Promo pool: 300 single-use codes, one code per registered email, non-transferable.
- Trial length: 90 days; trial start is first activation date.
- Code redemption window: must be activated by 2028-01-31; unused codes will be revoked after that date.
- Per-company cap: up to 5 activations per company domain to limit bulk redemptions.

Logan — please generate the CSV and upload to the secure folder, then share the link to Susan's marketing contact with write-only access. Include a short README in the upload with redemption instructions and the HubSpot campaign tag we agreed on (utm_source=devstreams&utm_campaign=cloudcode_nov2027).

Susan — by replying ACK to this email you'll accept the conditions and we'll proceed.

Thanks,
Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Partnerships Operations, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com
(attachment: promo_codes_CloudCode_300.csv)



--- THREAD METADATA / ACTION LOG ---
Provisional timeline: attendee CSV due Nov 18 (external) -> codes generated Nov 22 -> follow-up email distribution Nov 30 post-event.
Issuance artifact: promo_codes_CloudCode_300.csv (uploaded to secure share).
HubSpot: HS-Deal-48521 updated with promo tag.

title: Discovery recap + light ops handhold — next steps
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Samir Nair <samir@simplidata.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Discovery recap + light ops handhold — next steps

Hi Samir / Priya —

Thanks again for the discovery call today. Quick recap and proposed light next steps so we keep momentum without blocking your infra team: 

- What we heard: observability pipeline (ingest -> semantic search) is the primary driver; latency targets 150ms median, retention policy 90 days.
- Suggested pilot scope: 1 endpoint, non-prod data, Redwood-hosted inference for the first 2 weeks to validate latency and token-cost profile.
- Potential blockers: network egress policy and key management (KMS).

Proposed next steps:
1) I can spin up a scoped trial instance for you and provide a short ops checklist (15–30m) to walk through the minimal network/KMS changes.
2) Monica can share a short SOW draft and a quick timeline (1 pager).
3) If you prefer, we can do a 30-minute office-hours session next week to unblock infra.

Attachments: redwood-light-ops-checklist.pdf (Attachment stub) — light checklist covering ports, VPC peering, KMS rules.
Also attached an initial SOW draft for visibility: initial-sow-draft.docx (Attachment stub).

Proposed times for the office-hours slot: Thu 9/9 10:00–10:30 PDT or Fri 9/10 14:00–14:30 PDT — what works?

Thanks — happy to keep this light.

—Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

title: Follow-up: buy committee roles, latency buckets & hosting preference clarifications
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Kyle Barnes <kyle@medflow.health>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: buy committee roles, latency buckets & hosting preference clarifications

Kyle — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and a couple of clarifying asks so we can align the next internal review on your side before bringing in procurement/security: 

Summary of what I heard
- Primary use case: inference for clinical triage suggestions (short prompts, bursts around shift changes).
- Initial throughput: ~20 reqs/sec peak, expected to grow to 80 reqs/sec within 6–9 months.
- Preliminary latency target: P95 <= 200ms for prompt size ~128 tokens (we flagged 100ms as aspirational).

Clarifying asks (helps us propose a best-fit hosting + SLA option):
1) Which person on your buy committee holds budget authority (economic buyer)? You mentioned a finance sign-off — can you share name and preferred timeline for a decision?
2) For security gating: do you need a full SOC 2 package + customer-specific evidence, or will an SOC2 attestation + SOC2 extract be sufficient for initial procurement?
3) Deployment preference: do you want us to propose (A) Hosted API (fastest path), (B) Dedicated reserved pool, or (C) Private VPC? Any hard constraints on network egress or on-prem requirement?
4) SLA expectations: you called out 99.9% as a starting point — is that a must-have, or is 99.5% acceptable if we offer stronger latency guarantees and a 4 hr P1 response?

Next steps I propose
- I’ll send a short matrix mapping hosting modes vs. expected P95 latency/cost per 1M tokens and what we can commit to for initial pilots.
- If you can share the economic buyer contact and procurement cadence, I’ll draft an initial mutual-action plan (MAP) and a procurement checklist for your team.

Attachments I’ll include with the next note: latency vs cost matrix, example MAP template, and SOC2 extract.

Best —
Mateo Alvarez
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0182

---

P.S. Vanessa / Neha — looping you for visibility. Vanessa, can you prepare the hosting comparison (hosted / dedicated / private) for the P95 buckets Kyle referenced? Neha, please flag any mandatory evidence items we should pre-attach to the MAP (e.g., encryption at rest certs, retained logs period).

From: Kyle Barnes <kyle@medflow.health>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Ellen Shaw <ellen.shaw@medflow.health>, MedFlow Procurement <procurement@medflow.health>
Date: Mon, 23 Aug 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: buy committee roles, latency buckets & hosting preference clarifications

Hi Mateo — great summary, thanks. A few quick answers from our end: 

1) Economic buyer: Kyle Barnes (me) will sign off up to $250k annual. Anything above that needs a CFO loop (Daniel Park). Target decision window: 6 weeks, but we’d like a pilot live in ~4 weeks.

2) Security/evidence: for pilot procurement, SOC2 extract + attestation + network diagram should be fine. For GA/production we'll need full SOC2 report and a security questionnaire filled out. Ellen (cc) will confirm required attestation fields.

3) Hosting preference: Pilot — Hosted API is preferred for speed. For production we are leaning Dedicated (reserved) if latency/p95 holds — open to Private if legal flags data residency concerns.

4) SLA: 99.5% is acceptable for pilot if you can commit to 95th/99th/99.5th latency buckets and a P1 response within 4 hours. For production we may need 99.9% depending on contract value.

Procurement steps: procurement@medflow.health will intake a MAP and PO. They indicated their legal review is 10–14 business days once procurement has the MAP + risk artifacts.

I attached a short procurement checklist (see attachment) and a rough latency bucket doc (MedFlow_MVP_latency_matrix.xlsx) from the infra team.

Happy to jump on a 30 min follow-up with you + Ellen next week to sync on the MAP and evidence. What does your calendar look like the week of Aug 30?

Thanks,
Kyle Barnes
Sr. Product Manager — Clinical Ops | MedFlow Health
kyle@medflow.health | +1 617-555-9621

Attachment: MedFlow_procurement_instructions.pdf (pdf, 128KB)
Attachment: MedFlow_MVP_latency_matrix.xlsx (xlsx, 64KB)

To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 24 Aug 2027 08:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: buy committee roles, latency buckets & hosting preference clarifications

Mateo — quick internal note for the MAP draft. Based on Kyle's reply:

- Pilot proposal (4 weeks): Hosted API with a 2x burst buffer to handle shift-change spikes. Offer telemetry + prefix caching to lower per-token cost.
- Latency commitments to propose for pilot: P95 <= 200ms, P99 <= 350ms for 128-token prompts. Include a clause that we’ll run an initial 48-hour reliability soak to baseline numbers.
- Cost estimate: provide 3 pricing tiers (low/med/high) keyed to concurrency and caching level.
- Security attachments to include with MAP: SOC2-extract-2026.pdf, network diagram (we'll ask infra to produce), summary of encryption & access controls.

Neha — can you confirm whether the SOC2 extract we have includes the specific key controls MedFlow might ask for (e.g., KMS usage, role-based access audit trails)? If not, we'll mark those as 'available on request' and surface them to procurement.

I can draft the hosting matrix by EOD today so we can attach it to the MAP.

—Vanessa

3) Neha — can you confirm if we can surface the KMS and role-based access audit trails in short form for procurement? If we can, include that as an attachment so procurement can pre-seed legal.
4) Proposed follow-up meeting: Wed, 31 Aug 2027 10:00–10:30 PT — 30 min to review MAP and evidence. If that works share a calendar invite and I’ll include an agenda.

Kyle — we’ll keep the pilot SLA framing at 99.5% with latency buckets as Vanessa proposed. For production we’ll outline a path to 99.9% SLA with pricing/commitment thresholds.

title: Re: Throttled access vs Unlimited 30d trial — terms and pricing
preview:
From: Samir Rao <samir@claritydata.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Trial model access — throttled vs unlimited

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo this morning — we liked the latency and the KV caching behavior. We're kicking off a 30-day evaluation with our ML infra team but need to settle on access terms. Two asks from our side: 

1) Is it possible to run an "unlimited access" 30-day trial where our engineers can spin up concurrent calls for stress testing?
2) Alternatively, can Redwood provide a throttled plan (e.g., 25 concurrent reqs, token cap of 500k total) at a lower price so we can test latency under controlled load?

Budget note: Clarity's trial budget is constrained — if there is any temporary discount or a usage credit that smooths the cost during the 30-day window, that would help the case internally.

I've attached a rough usage projection (clarity-pilot-usage-sheet.csv). Can you propose a term sheet and any guardrails you'd recommend?

Appreciate it — Samir
Principal Data Engineer
Clarity Data

Attachment: clarity-pilot-usage-sheet.csv (CSV, 12KB)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@claritydata.com>
Cc: Tess Howard <tess@claritydata.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Jul 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Trial model access — throttled vs unlimited

Samir / Tess — thanks for the usage sheet and the thoughtful asks. Short answer: we can support both patterns but they have different pricing/operational tradeoffs. Below is a concise proposal and two paths so you can pick what aligns with your objectives.

Option A — Unlimited 30d trial (stress testing):
- Access: no artificial concurrency limits for 30 days, subject to fair-use and safety filters.
- Support: dedicated onboarding session + 24/5 Slack access.
- Cost: usage-billed at our hosted trial rate with a 40% one-time trial credit applied to invoices (applies only to usage, not to add-ons).
- Constraints: We require a signed short-form MSA and a refundable card hold for surge protection.

Option B — Throttled scaled trial (controlled load):
- Access: dedicated instance with enforced concurrency cap (25 concurrent), token cap 500k for the trial window.
- Support: architecture consultation to help you shape real-world tests.
- Cost: discounted flat trial bundle: $5,000 fixed for the month (covers up to 500k tokens and reserved concurrency). Overage priced at standard hosted rates.

Hybrid suggestion (common): Start with Option B for safe ramp, then provide a 3-day unlimited stress window near the end of the 30 days so your team can run the heavy tests without committing to full unlimited for the month. We can cap that stress window by a one-time surcharge or apply remaining credits.

Next steps:
- If you want Option A, I can circulate a short MSA + trial addendum and draft a refundable card hold doc.
- If Option B or Hybrid, confirm and we'll produce a one-page term sheet and reserve the instance.

I've attached a short term sheet (redwood-term-sheet-v1.pdf) and linked the HubSpot record for reference.

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-term-sheet-v1.pdf (PDF, 48KB)
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/contact/67890

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Trial model access — pricing options (need quick OK)

Karthik / Lauren — forwarding Samir's ask and the two-path proposal. TL;DR:

Subject: Trial model access — throttled vs unlimited

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo this morning — we liked the latency and the KV caching behavior. We're kicking off a 30-day evaluation with our ML infra team but need to settle on access terms. Two asks from our side: 

1) Is it possible to run an "unlimited access" 30-day trial where our engineers can spin up concurrent calls for stress testing?
2) Alternatively, can Redwood provide a throttled plan (e.g., 25 concurrent reqs, token cap of 500k total) at a lower price so we can test latency under controlled load?

Budget note: Clarity's trial budget is constrained — if there is any temporary discount or a usage credit that smooths the cost during the 30-day window, that would help the case internally.

I've attached a rough usage projection (clarity-pilot-usage-sheet.csv). Can you propose a term sheet and any guardrails you'd recommend?

Appreciate it — Samir
Principal Data Engineer
Clarity Data

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@claritydata.com>
Cc: Tess Howard <tess@claritydata.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Jul 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Trial model access — throttled vs unlimited

Samir / Tess — thanks for the usage sheet and the thoughtful asks. Short answer: we can support both patterns but they have different pricing/operational tradeoffs. Below is a concise proposal and two paths so you can pick what aligns with your objectives.

Option A — Unlimited 30d trial (stress testing):
- Access: no artificial concurrency limits for 30 days, subject to fair-use and safety filters.
- Support: dedicated onboarding session + 24/5 Slack access.
- Cost: usage-billed at our hosted trial rate with a 40% one-time trial credit applied to invoices (applies only to usage, not to add-ons).
- Constraints: We require a signed short-form MSA and a refundable card hold for surge protection.

Option B — Throttled scaled trial (controlled load):
- Access: dedicated instance with enforced concurrency cap (25 concurrent), token cap 500k for the trial window.
- Support: architecture consultation to help you shape real-world tests.
- Cost: discounted flat trial bundle: $5,000 fixed for the month (covers up to 500k tokens and reserved concurrency). Overage priced at standard hosted rates.

Hybrid suggestion (common): Start with Option B for safe ramp, then provide a 3-day unlimited stress window near the end of the 30 days so your team can run the heavy tests without committing to full unlimited for the month. We can cap that stress window by a one-time surcharge or apply remaining credits.

Next steps:
- If you want Option A, I can circulate a short MSA + trial addendum and draft a refundable card hold doc.
- If Option B or Hybrid, confirm and we'll produce a one-page term sheet and reserve the instance.

I've attached a short term sheet (redwood-term-sheet-v1.pdf) and linked the HubSpot record for reference.

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-term-sheet-v1.pdf (PDF, 48KB)
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/contact/67890

title: Definition of Done orchestration / test handoff for pilot
preview:
From: Sara Klein <sara.k@healthsync.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot kickoff — need clear Definition of Done and acceptance tests

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for the kickoff yesterday — the team here is excited to move to a short pilot. Before we sign off on a Mutual Action Plan we need a concise, operational Definition of Done (DoD) for the evaluation phase that we can take to procurement and engineering. Specifically: 

- A short checklist of pass/fail acceptance tests (functional and non-functional) with numeric thresholds where possible.
- Named owners for each test on both sides.
- Any required artifacts (sample logs, evidence formats, QA prompts).
- Expected timeline (start, smoke, full-run, cutover decision point).

Procurement also wants SLA targets and retention windows (audit logs, telemetry). Can you send a crisp DoD matrix and a suggested handoff plan by EOD Wednesday?

We’ve attached our standard pilot NDAs and procurement info if that helps.

Thanks,
Sara
Sara Klein
Director, Solutions — HealthSync

Attachments: HS_NDA_signed.pdf (attached by HealthSync)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Sara Klein <sara.k@healthsync.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2027 11:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot kickoff — need clear Definition of Done and acceptance tests

Sara — thanks for the clarity. Attached is a first-pass DoD matrix and a short MAP-style handoff. High level below; attached: DoD_matrix_v1.xlsx and MAP_timeline_v1.pdf.

Executive summary (short):
1) Core success criteria (must pass all):
  - Functional: Intent extraction & response correctness >= 92% on curated eval set (owner: HealthSync QA, verify: Redwood QA harness).
  - Latency: 95th percentile inference < 350ms per request under pilot traffic (owner: Redwood infra).
  - Throughput: sustained 60 requests/min with no more than 1% error rate for 30m window (owner: Redwood infra).
  - Data controls: all PHI/TI routed through private tenant; audit log writes for every inference event (owner: HealthSync security + Redwood platform).
  - Safety/Concordance: Top-1 hallucination spot-check rate < 3% on blind sample (owner: HealthSync product).

2) Acceptance tests and artifacts:
  - Functional smoke: 200-sample curated set run end-to-end; pass if accuracy >= 92% and no critical regressions. (owner: HealthSync QA; evidence: DoD_matrix_v1.xlsx -> results tab).
  - Latency/throughput soak: 2 x 30m ramp tests with K6 driver, capture p95/p99 and error traces. (owner: Redwood infra; evidence: telemetry snapshot + p95/p99 CSV).
  - Audit & retention verification: proof of audit-write delivery for 7 days (we can extend to 12 if required). (owner: HealthSync Sec + Redwood Eng).
  - Security checklist: SOC2 items requested in procurement packet; we’ll provide audit extracts for the pilot window. (owner: Redwood security).

3) Owners & handoff:
  - HealthSync: Product owner (S. Klein), QA lead (TBD), Security (procurement).
  - Redwood: AE (Marissa), Solutions Eng (Mateo), Platform Lead (Monica).

Proposed timeline (compact pilot):
  - Week 0 (this week): finalize DoD and procurement checklist.
  - Week 1: infra provisioning and test data exchange.
  - Week 2: smoke runs (functional + latency).
  - Week 3: extended soak and evidence collection.
  - Decision checkpoint: Day 21 — green to proceed to pilot cutover if DoD met.

Open items I need from you to finalize the MAP: a named QA contact, confirmation on audit log retention (7 vs 12 months), and any procurement IDs. I can update the DoD matrix based on your feedback.

Cheers,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0172

Attachments: DoD_matrix_v1.xlsx (DoD matrix, tests + thresholds), MAP_timeline_v1.pdf (timeline & owners)

Forwarding Sara's ask — want to align internal owners and quick action items. Short notes: 

- Mateo: take ownership of functional test harness + result packaging. Target: ship test-run report template by Wed EOD.
- Monica: own latency/soak plan and p95 instrumentation; coordinate infra window W1. Also confirm whether we can extend audit retention to 12 months for HS pilot (there may be fee/ops implications).

Please reply with concrete owners (name + email) and any blockers. I’ll fold the responses into a mutual action plan we can send to Sara.

--
Marissa

From: Sara Klein <sara.k@healthsync.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthSync Procurement <procurement@healthsync.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot kickoff — need clear Definition of Done and acceptance tests

Marissa — thanks for the quick turnaround and the matrix. A few notes from procurement/security that are blockers unless we update: 

1) Audit retention: procurement requires 12 months for audit logs for any pilot that touches customer PHI. Can Redwood support that as part of the pilot or do you need a scoped exception?

Subject: Re: Pilot kickoff — need clear Definition of Done and acceptance tests

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for the kickoff yesterday — the team here is excited to move to a short pilot. Before we sign off on a Mutual Action Plan we need a concise, operational Definition of Done (DoD) for the evaluation phase that we can take to procurement and engineering. Specifically: 

- A short checklist of pass/fail acceptance tests (functional and non-functional) with numeric thresholds where possible.
- Named owners for each test on both sides.
- Any required artifacts (sample logs, evidence formats, QA prompts).
- Expected timeline (start, smoke, full-run, cutover decision point).

Procurement also wants SLA targets and retention windows (audit logs, telemetry). Can you send a crisp DoD matrix and a suggested handoff plan by EOD Wednesday?

We’ve attached our standard pilot NDAs and procurement info if that helps.

Thanks,
Sara

Director, Solutions — HealthSync

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>

Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2027 11:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot kickoff — need clear Definition of Done and acceptance tests

Sara — thanks for the clarity. Attached is a first-pass DoD matrix and a short MAP-style handoff. High level below; attached: DoD_matrix_v1.xlsx and MAP_timeline_v1.pdf.

Executive summary (short):
1) Core success criteria (must pass all):
  - Functional: Intent extraction & response correctness >= 92% on curated eval set (owner: HealthSync QA, verify: Redwood QA harness).
  - Latency: 95th percentile inference < 350ms per request under pilot traffic (owner: Redwood infra).
  - Throughput: sustained 60 requests/min with no more than 1% error rate for 30m window (owner: Redwood infra).
  - Data controls: all PHI/TI routed through private tenant; audit log writes for every inference event (owner: HealthSync security + Redwood platform).
  - Safety/Concordance: Top-1 hallucination spot-check rate < 3% on blind sample (owner: HealthSync product).

2) Acceptance tests and artifacts:
  - Functional smoke: 200-sample curated set run end-to-end; pass if accuracy >= 92% and no critical regressions. (owner: HealthSync QA; evidence: DoD_matrix_v1.xlsx -> results tab).
  - Latency/throughput soak: 2 x 30m ramp tests with K6 driver, capture p95/p99 and error traces. (owner: Redwood infra; evidence: telemetry snapshot + p95/p99 CSV).
  - Audit & retention verification: proof of audit-write delivery for 7 days (we can extend to 12 if required). (owner: HealthSync Sec + Redwood Eng).
  - Security checklist: SOC2 items requested in procurement packet; we’ll provide audit extracts for the pilot window. (owner: Redwood security).

3) Owners & handoff:
  - HealthSync: Product owner (S. Klein), QA lead (TBD), Security (procurement).
  - Redwood: AE (Marissa), Solutions Eng (Mateo), Platform Lead (Monica).

Proposed timeline (compact pilot):
  - Week 0 (this week): finalize DoD and procurement checklist.
  - Week 1: infra provisioning and test data exchange.
  - Week 2: smoke runs (functional + latency).
  - Week 3: extended soak and evidence collection.
  - Decision checkpoint: Day 21 — green to proceed to pilot cutover if DoD met.

Open items I need from you to finalize the MAP: a named QA contact, confirmation on audit log retention (7 vs 12 months), and any procurement IDs. I can update the DoD matrix based on your feedback.

Cheers,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0172

Attachments: DoD_matrix_v1.xlsx (DoD matrix, tests + thresholds), MAP_timeline_v1.pdf (timeline & owners)

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Attached is our initial DPA redline (track changes) — counsel at MKCorp added a few edits and questions around subprocessors and transfer mechanisms.

High-level asks from our side:
- Confirm whether Redwood can sign the EU SCCs or if you require a separate addendum for EU->US transfers.
- Clarify the subprocessor notification process and whether MKCorp will be able to review the subprocessor list quarterly.
- Request for a 24/7 incident escalation contact and a more specific SLA credit metric for prolonged outages.

We'd like to get this wrapped in the next 10 business days; happy to jump on a quick call with counsel if helpful.

Cc: Sarah.Lewis@mkcorp.com
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Draft DPA and initial redlines for MKCorp

Neha / Karthik — forwarding MKCorp's redlines. Please see counsel questions in the attached. Quick context: MKCorp is evaluating Redwood for a multi-region deployment (EU primary), deal size target ~$300k ARR first year. Procurement wants clarity on transfer mechanism and subprocessor cadence.

Can you please:
- Neha: provide suggested responses and any proposed alternate language (inline) for the subprocessor clause and transfer text (SCCs vs addendum). Also advise on incident contact language — we usually provide a 24/7 PagerDuty contact but MKCorp is asking for an SLA credit threshold.
- Karthik: if we accept any of the commercial asks (SLA credits), note the commercial impact.

I flagged the redline where MKCorp changed Section 4.2 (subprocessors) and the EU transfer clause.

Thanks —
Mateo
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
Attachments: MKCorp_DPA_Redline_v1.pdf

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Sarah Lewis <sarah.lewis@mkcorp.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Draft DPA and initial redlines for MKCorp
To: mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
Cc: counsel@mkcorplegal.com
(see above)


From: Neha.Kapoor@redwood.com
To: mateo.alvarez@redwood.com, Karthik.Iyer@redwood.com
Cc: Sarah.Lewis@mkcorp.com
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Draft DPA and initial redlines for MKCorp — privacy counsel comments

Thanks for the ping, Mateo — reviewed MKCorp's redlines and added counsel-level comments inline. Main points below (copied to MKCorp for transparency):

1) EU transfer mechanism
- Recommendation: we accept execution of the EU SCCs (module two) and provide a signed SCC Annex rather than a separate proprietary addendum. This aligns with our standard approach and keeps execution simple for customers.
- Suggested language: we will append the most recent EC SCCs as an Annex; we will notify customers of any updates and obtain signatures where required.

2) Subprocessors (Section 4.2)
- MKCorp asks for quarterly review rights. We typically publish an up-to-date subprocessor list and notify customers 30 days in advance of material changes. Offering quarterly review sessions is operationally heavy.
- Compromise language proposed: "Redwood will publish and maintain an up-to-date list of subprocessors and provide advance notice of material additions. Upon reasonable request, Redwood will provide a summary of subprocessor categories and will coordinate a quarterly summary call with MKCorp's security team, limited to once per quarter, unless otherwise agreed."

3) Incident response and SLA credits
- We provide a 24/7 incident contact and can include an escalation contact in the DPA. Regarding SLA credits: our standard platform SLA credit structure is in the commercial schedule; we should avoid adding new credit mechanics into the DPA. Proposed response: confirm the 24/7 escalation contact and reference the existing SLA schedule for credit calculations.

4) Data localization and retention
- MKCorp inserted a clause requesting data residency guarantees in EU. We cannot guarantee physical residency without a dedicated private deployment. Propose clarifying: for hosted customers, data will be stored in the customer-designated region where available; any region-specific residency guarantees require a dedicated/private deployment and a separate addendum.

5) Audit and evidence requests
- We can provide SOC 2 Type II summary and a scoping statement; for full audit we require standard scoping/fees. Propose adding a line about providing SOC 2 report under NDA and a path for on-site audits subject to notice and cost.

Next steps I recommend:
- I will prepare an inline redline with the language above and return it to Mateo by EOD.
- Mateo, if MKCorp needs faster turnaround, offer a 30-minute review with counsel and me to walk through the compromises.

Neha Kapoor
Senior Privacy Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: MKCorp_DPA_Redline_v1_with_privacy_comments.docx


From: mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
To: Sarah.Lewis@mkcorp.com, counsel@mkcorplegal.com
Cc: Neha.Kapoor@redwood.com, Karthik.Iyer@redwood.com
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 15:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MKCorp DPA redlines, EU transfer notes, and e-sign path

Sarah — thanks for the fast turnaround and for looping counsel. Neha's suggestions look good to us; I've attached the updated redline (minor edits to subprocessor notice and SCC language).

To keep the timeline tight: if MKCorp is comfortable with the proposed SCC annex approach and the subprocessor compromise, I'll route this for e-sign today. Proposed signing path: Redwood + MKCorp sign DPA (with SCC Annex) via DocuSign; SOC 2 report exchange to follow under NDA.

Please confirm you'd like me to send the DocuSign envelope and I will include the SCC Annex as a separate attachment in the envelope. Link below will be the live envelope once I initiate it.

DocuSign preview (will send envelope upon confirmation): https://app.docusign.com/envelope/EX-1234567890

If MKCorp requires different SLA credit language, flag that now — otherwise we'll keep SLA credit mechanics in the commercial schedule per Neha's note.

Thanks —
Mateo Alvarez
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0132
