title: Patch rework and split deliveries for SF Summit
preview:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: ops@sewnright.com
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Patch rework request + options for split delivery to SF Summit

Hi Anna / SewnRight team,

Quick heads-up on the embroidered patches we received on Tuesday — two issues spotted in the first production run:
- Thread color is slightly too light compared to the approved proof (see attached patch_proof_v3.pdf).
- Finished diameter is ~2.5\" but spec was 3\" (looks like a scale setting during digitizing).

We have 500 patches total. SF Summit logistics are tight; we need at least 400 to arrive at the venue by 06/28, and the remaining 100 can go to our sponsor lounge pickup on 06/25.

Please advise:
1) Can you rework the existing 500 (repair/stitch-over) or do we need a full remake?
2) Lead times and incremental costs for (A) full remake of 500, (B) remake 400 + repair 100, (C) rush expedite fees to meet the 06/25/06/28 split deliveries.
3) Shipping options and estimated costs for a split delivery (400 -> EventWorks / Moscone Center; 100 -> Redwood sponsor lounge address listed in attached CSV).

Attachments: patch_proof_v3.pdf; production_photo_batch01.jpg (sample close-up).

We need a quote by EOD today if possible so we can get a PO out. Marissa will handle billing.

Thanks,
Dylan Brooks
Field Marketing Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Anna Rossi <ops@sewnright.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 13:45:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Patch rework request + options for split delivery to SF Summit

Hi Dylan — thanks for the clear notes and the photos. We pulled the first run and inspected the batch.

Short summary and options:

- Cause: digitizing scale was set to 90% in the production file which explains the 2.5\" finish. Thread color is a dye lot mismatch.

Options and lead times (from receipt of PO and approval):
A) Full remake of 500 (recommended for uniformity) — 10 business days standard, $3.25/patch, $1,625 material/setup fee.
B) Remake 400 + repair 100 (repair = hand-stitch + color touch) — 8 business days for the 400 batch + 2 days for repairs, $3.40/patch for remake portion (smaller batch), $2.00/patch repair labor, no additional setup fee for the 100 repairs.
C) Repair-only (not recommended) — variable finish quality, 5 business days, $2.40/patch.

Expedite: add 25% to per-unit cost to move to 72-hour turnaround if we can start immediately.

Shipping options:
- Single consolidated shipment to EventWorks Logistics (they'll handle final movement to Moscone) — cheaper, standard 2-day freight.
- Split shipments (we can split at our warehouse):
   • 400 -> EventWorks / Moscone Center (arrive by 06/28): freight $185 (LTL).
   • 100 -> Redwood Sponsor Lounge address (arrive by 06/25): UPS expedited $75.

Attached: SewnRight_quote_20270620.pdf (breakdown + expedite line), and production_photo_batch01.jpg (close-up).

If you confirm option B (400 remake + 100 repair) and expedite, estimated total = $1,360 (remake 400) + $200 (repair 100) + expedite 25% (~$390) + shipping ~$260 = ~ $2,210. Final will be exact on PO.

We can include hang-tags and numbered batch labels at $0.20/hangtag and $0.05/label.

Please send PO and the two delivery addresses and we will queue production.

Best,
Anna Rossi
Operations Lead
SewnRight Apparel
Attachments: SewnRight_quote_20270620.pdf, production_photo_batch01.jpg


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: ops@sewnright.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Jun 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Patch rework request + options for split delivery to SF Summit

- Confirm remake 400 size to exact 3\" finished diameter and thread color Pantone 19-4010 TCX (matches proof).

We have 500 patches total. SF Summit logistics are tight; we need at least 400 to arrive at the venue by 06/28, and the remaining 100 can go to our sponsor lounge pickup on 06/25.

Please advise:
1) Can you rework the existing 500 (repair/stitch-over) or do we need a full remake?
2) Lead times and incremental costs for (A) full remake of 500, (B) remake 400 + repair 100, (C) rush expedite fees to meet the 06/25/06/28 split deliveries.
3) Shipping options and estimated costs for a split delivery (400 -> EventWorks / Moscone Center; 100 -> Redwood sponsor lounge address listed in attached CSV).

From: Anna Rossi <ops@sewnright.com>

Date: Fri, 18 Jun 2027 13:45:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Patch rework request + options for split delivery to SF Summit

Hi Dylan — thanks for the clear notes and the photos. We pulled the first run and inspected the batch.

Short summary and options:

- Cause: digitizing scale was set to 90% in the production file which explains the 2.5\" finish. Thread color is a dye lot mismatch.

Options and lead times (from receipt of PO and approval):
A) Full remake of 500 (recommended for uniformity) — 10 business days standard, $3.25/patch, $1,625 material/setup fee.
B) Remake 400 + repair 100 (repair = hand-stitch + color touch) — 8 business days for the 400 batch + 2 days for repairs, $3.40/patch for remake portion (smaller batch), $2.00/patch repair labor, no additional setup fee for the 100 repairs.
C) Repair-only (not recommended) — variable finish quality, 5 business days, $2.40/patch.

Expedite: add 25% to per-unit cost to move to 72-hour turnaround if we can start immediately.

Shipping options:
- Single consolidated shipment to EventWorks Logistics (they'll handle final movement to Moscone) — cheaper, standard 2-day freight.
- Split shipments (we can split at our warehouse):
   • 400 -> EventWorks / Moscone Center (arrive by 06/28): freight $185 (LTL).
   • 100 -> Redwood Sponsor Lounge address (arrive by 06/25): UPS expedited $75.

Attached: SewnRight_quote_20270620.pdf (breakdown + expedite line), and production_photo_batch01.jpg (close-up).

If you confirm option B (400 remake + 100 repair) and expedite, estimated total = $1,360 (remake 400) + $200 (repair 100) + expedite 25% (~$390) + shipping ~$260 = ~ $2,210. Final will be exact on PO.

We can include hang-tags and numbered batch labels at $0.20/hangtag and $0.05/label.

Please send PO and the two delivery addresses and we will queue production.

Best,
Anna Rossi

SewnRight Apparel

title: Fiscal lane validation & approval handoff
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@astra.health>, Lauren Kim <lauren.kim@astra.health>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fiscal lane validation & approval handoff

Hi Samir / Lauren —

Thanks again for the demo this morning. Per our discussion, I wanted to capture the budget/pricing items so procurement has a clear path forward. Short recap + asks: 

- Quick recap: Astra is evaluating a pilot for in-product summarization + triage (expected traffic: 4–6M tokens/month initially).
- Target unit economics: you mentioned a target in the ballpark of $900–$1,200 per 1M tokens (you said finance is comfortable up to $1,000/1M but can stretch).
- Procurement thresholds: you flagged that internal PO process kicks in around ~$25k and anything above ~$200k needs director approval. Please confirm exact thresholds for us.

Immediate asks / proposed next steps: 
1) Confirm which monthly band you expect (please pick one): 1) 1–3M 2) 3–6M 3) 6–12M
2) Confirm preferred payment lane: credit card (fast), PO / Net30, or contract with reserved capacity.
3) If PO path, confirm minimal PO amount procurement will accept (e.g., $25k).

Attached I’m dropping our redwood-pricing-v2.pdf and a short procurement-checklist to show standard lanes and timelines. If it helps, Jordan can join a short procurement sync with your finance/procurement team — he’s looped here.

Goal: get a quick procurement agreement so we can spin up a pilot environment and lock pricing for the first 30–60 days.

Thanks —

Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-pricing-v2.pdf (pricing summary, model tiers) | procurement-checklist.docx (procurement lanes + required docs)

From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@astra.health>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Kim <lauren.kim@astra.health>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fiscal lane validation & approval handoff

Mateo — thanks for the clear notes. Quick responses inline.

- Monthly band: after checking with product, we are forecasting 3–6M tokens/month during the initial pilot window. That may scale later but let’s qualify the pilot at that band.
- Unit economics: Finance indicated they can approve up to ~$1,000 per 1M tokens for a pilot, provided there is a defined spend cap and clear deliverables.
- Procurement thresholds: Procurement told me they typically require a formal PO for any spend >= $25,000. For anything over $50,000 we need director-level signoff; over $200,000 triggers a multi-stakeholder review and longer lead times.

We’re leaning toward a PO / Net30 route for the pilot. Can you confirm: if we sign a PO at ~ $30k (covering the first 3 months), would Redwood accept standard Net30 terms and provide an invoice/statement? Also curious about a short-term discount if we commit to a 3-month pilot and then convert to a reserved commit.

I can schedule a procurement call with our procurement lead (procurement@astra.health) and finance (Lauren) tomorrow afternoon if that helps.

—Samir
Samir Patel
Head of Product, Astra Health
samir.patel@astra.health

On Mon, May 17, 2027 at 3:02 PM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message attached)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@astra.health>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Lauren Kim <lauren.kim@astra.health>, procurement@astra.health
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fiscal lane validation & approval handoff

Samir / Mateo — looping in procurement lane details and options so expectations are explicit. Thanks for the call, Samir.

Options (short):
- Self-serve / credit card (recommended for < $25k). Fastest to start, no PO required.
- PO / Net30 (typical for $25k – $200k). Redwood will accept a PO and Net30 invoices; we can issue an invoice on receipt of the PO and provide weekly usage statements.
- Reserved / Dedicated (recommended if you expect sustained > $200k/month). Reserved capacity comes with a different pricing band and SLA; requires a short contract and billing commit.

Pilot offer (for clarity):
- For a 3-month pilot at 3–6M tokens/month we can do a pilot PO for $30k covering month 1–2 burn with a consumption true-up at the end of month 2. If you convert to a 6-month reserved commit after the pilot we can apply a credit equal to 10% of pilot invoices toward the reserved commitment.

Procurement checklist summary (also attached):
- Required from customer for PO lane: Company legal name, remit-to address, W-9 / VAT info (if requested), contact for PO entry, expected PO number format.
- Typical lead time: 2–7 business days for PO issuance; director review can add 7–14 days.

If that works, Mateo can join the procurement call and I’ll bring a short slide with the PO flow and billing examples.

Thanks —

Jordan Blake
Sr. Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

> On Tue, May 18, 2027 at 9:14 AM Samir Patel <samir.patel@astra.health> wrote:
> > (Samir’s message)


From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@astra.health>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Kim <lauren.kim@astra.health>, procurement@astra.health
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fiscal lane validation & approval handoff

Thanks — everything looks sensible. Summary and next steps from our side: 

- We will plan the pilot at 3–6M tokens/month and target the PO path. Procurement confirmed they will raise a PO in the $30k–$40k range to cover initial pilot burn; director review not required for that band.
- Finance (Lauren) confirmed Net30 is acceptable. I’ve added procurement@astra.health to this thread and asked them to prepare the PO template.
- Action items: 
  - procurement@astra.health to send PO template + expected timeline (procurement)
  - Mateo / Jordan: join a 30-minute procurement sync tomorrow at 2pm PT (Samir will send invite)
  - Mateo: please attach a sample invoice and short billing cadence example in the calendar invite (one pager is fine)

We’re aiming to have a PO in hand by next week. Appreciate the flexibility and the pilot credit language — that should help expedite approval.

Samir

--

- redwood-pricing-v2.pdf (pricing summary; model tiers and per-1M token lanes)
- procurement-checklist.docx (purchase lanes, required documents, contact points)

title: Embedded tracing sample app + ingestion cost model: pilot scoping
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Samir Joshi <samir.j@datadog.com>, Praja Rao <prao@grafana.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Embedded tracing sample app + ingestion cost model: pilot scoping

Hi Samir / Priya —

Thanks again for the quick sync last week. As discussed on the product side, Redwood would like to run a short co-engineering pilot to validate:

- an "embedded tracing" reference app (language + SDK config) that shows realistic span density for LLM inference workloads,
- the ingestion footprint (bytes/min, spans/min) into Datadog/Grafana backends with OTLP over HTTP and OTLP/grpc, and
- an ingestion cost model that we can use to size agent configs and retention for customers.

High-level ask: can Datadog + Grafana participate in a 3-week pilot where we co-develop the sample app, run controlled synthetic loads, and produce a short SOW with PS time split and success criteria? Goal: quick technical validation that gives sales a safe prescriptive recipe for customers integrating Redwood inference telemetry.

I attached a short spec (redwood_tracing_sample_spec.md) and a first-pass SOW (pilot_statement_of_work_v0.3.pdf) — please review the scope and cost allocation table in the spreadsheet.

Proposed kickoff: Mon 17 May, 10:00 PT (30–45m). If that works, Ben / Laura and Karthik will join from our side.

Action items I think we need to confirm before kickoff:
- partner confirms contact + sandbox account for ingestion testing,
- agreed list of SDK variants (python/node/java/go), sampling scenarios, and span attributes to capture,
- security/filtering constraints for PII-sensitive traces, and
- PS split and who signs the SOW (we can use DocuSign).

Thanks — happy to be flexible on dates.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Sr. Partner Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood_tracing_sample_spec.md (text/markdown)]
[Attachment: pilot_statement_of_work_v0.3.pdf (application/pdf)]


From: Samir Joshi <samir.j@datadog.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Priya Rao <prao@grafana.com>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Embedded tracing sample app + ingestion cost model: pilot scoping

Mateo —

Appreciate the clear spec. Datadog can participate in the 3-week co-engineering pilot; we can provide a sandbox ingest endpoint and a technical lead (me) plus an SRE (Kelsey). A couple quick clarifying questions before kickoff:

1) For "span density" can you give an expected range (spans/sec per request) for the sample LLM flows you plan to simulate? We want to pre-configure sampling and indexing rules.
2) Are you expecting secure ingest (mTLS) or standard API key auth in the sandbox? mTLS will add a little setup time.
3) On retention: do you want us to model 7/30/90 day scenarios in the cost sheet, or should we focus on ingest/day only?

We can do the proposed kickoff Mon 17 May 10:00 PT. I'll invite Kelsey and our instrumentation PM.

Also: small ask from legal — can we set a non-production data handling note in the SOW that Redwood will not send customer PII to partner sandboxes? We have a short template clause we can share.

Thanks,
Samir Joshi
Senior Partner Engineer, Datadog
[Signature: samir.j@datadog.com | +1 (415) 555-0129]


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Joshi <samir.j@datadog.com>, Priya Rao <prao@grafana.com>
Date: Thu, 13 May 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Embedded tracing sample app + ingestion cost model: pilot scoping

Looping in a few internal notes before kickoff:

- Karthik, please own the sample app repo layout + CI harness (light-weight load generator that can emit common LLM conversational patterns). Deliverable: repo + README by Fri 14 May.
- Ben, please draft a short slide (1–2) that explains how customers consume the prescriptive recipe post-pilot (sell motion + enablement).
- Mateo, verify that the spec explicitly excludes production keys and any customer data — legal will need that for the SOW.

I'll join the kickoff to help align commercial ask and confirm PS splits; we'll aim for a 60/40 split in PS time depending on who owns the heavy-lift work — final numbers after technical scope locked.

Quick reminder for kickoff: attach the ingestion-cost-projections.xlsx to the calendar invite so partners can pre-populate numbers.

Laura Bennett
Director, Alliances and Partnerships
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Rao <prao@grafana.com>

Subject: Embedded tracing sample app + ingestion cost model: pilot scoping

Hi Samir / Priya —

Thanks again for the quick sync last week. As discussed on the product side, Redwood would like to run a short co-engineering pilot to validate:

- an "embedded tracing" reference app (language + SDK config) that shows realistic span density for LLM inference workloads,
- the ingestion footprint (bytes/min, spans/min) into Datadog/Grafana backends with OTLP over HTTP and OTLP/grpc, and
- an ingestion cost model that we can use to size agent configs and retention for customers.

High-level ask: can Datadog + Grafana participate in a 3-week pilot where we co-develop the sample app, run controlled synthetic loads, and produce a short SOW with PS time split and success criteria? Goal: quick technical validation that gives sales a safe prescriptive recipe for customers integrating Redwood inference telemetry.

I attached a short spec (redwood_tracing_sample_spec.md) and a first-pass SOW (pilot_statement_of_work_v0.3.pdf) — please review the scope and cost allocation table in the spreadsheet.

Proposed kickoff: Mon 17 May, 10:00 PT (30–45m). If that works, Ben / Laura and Karthik will join from our side.

Action items I think we need to confirm before kickoff:
- partner confirms contact + sandbox account for ingestion testing,
- agreed list of SDK variants (python/node/java/go), sampling scenarios, and span attributes to capture,
- security/filtering constraints for PII-sensitive traces, and
- PS split and who signs the SOW (we can use DocuSign).

Thanks — happy to be flexible on dates.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Sr. Partner Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

1) For "span density" can you give an expected range (spans/sec per request) for the sample LLM flows you plan to simulate? We want to pre-configure sampling and indexing rules.

title: Request: provisional capacity uplift and phased invoicing for pilot
preview:
From: Kirti Rao <kirti@nimhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:12:00Z
Subject: Request: provisional capacity uplift and phased invoicing for pilot

Hi Mateo,

We're running into higher-than-expected traffic during our week-3 pilot window. Yesterday we spiked ~3.5x above our forecasted token usage and hit the current quota cap mid-day, which throttled a subset of our ingestion pipelines. We have a hard customer deadline next Friday and need a short-term uplift to avoid lost telemetry.

Can Redwood provisionally increase our throughput ceiling by ~2x for 72 hours while we stabilize? Ideally we'd: 
- Get the uplift within the next 48 hours
- Have billing for the uplift phased so we don't get a single large overrun invoice (we can accept a provisional charge + final true-up)

I'm attaching a small usage snapshot from the last 7 days to show the spike. Open to a one-off credit in return for committing to a 12-month minimum if that helps.

Thanks,
Kirti
Director of Integrations
Nimbus Health

Attachment: usage_snapshot_q2.csv (CSV, 38KB)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Kirti Rao <kirti@nimhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T10:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Request: provisional capacity uplift and phased invoicing for pilot

Kirti — thanks for the heads up and the CSV. Sorry to hear the pilot hit that spike. We can move quickly here.

Two parts: capacity and billing.
- Capacity: I can request an emergency temporary QPS/headroom bump for 72 hours. That will need approval from sales ops to allocate short-term capacity.
- Billing: we can offer a phased approach: an upfront provisional fee to cover immediate extra capacity and then a final true-up against actual usage at the end of the month. We can also apply a small one-time credit if you convert to a 12-month commitment.

Looping in Ben to propose the provisional uplift and the provisional-rate sheet. Ben — can you propose the uplift parameters (rate, credit, DocuSign for a short amendment) so we can get Nimbus back online within 48 hours?

Mateo
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

On 2027-05-09 08:12, Kirti Rao wrote:

title: Pilot packaging: concierge pilot / capacity credits vs tier
preview:
From: Alice Chen <alice@starlithealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Patrick O'Neill <patrick.oneill@starlithealth.com>, Legal Ops <legal@starlithealth.com>
Date: Fri, 7 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Packaging question for our pilot — need options

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We want to run a short pilot to validate inference quality and integration effort before committing to anything long-term. I attached our expected usage profile (pilot_usage_sheet.csv) — it's small but spiky: roughly 250k tokens/month with occasional bursts up to 100k tokens/day during training windows.

Two things we want clarity on:
- Whether a short reserved-tier pilot (3 months) makes sense versus simply doing a consumption-based pilot (capacity credits).
- What a "concierge" add-on (onboarding + weekly tuning) would look like as a priced line item vs being bundled into a pilot discount.

We also need an estimate for discounts and any language you'd need on the commitment side. Legal will review but wants a ballpark first. Can you propose 2–3 packaging options and the tradeoffs?

Appreciate it —
Alice
Principal Product Manager
Starlit Health

Attachments: pilot_usage_sheet.csv (CSV, 12KB)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Alice Chen <alice@starlithealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 7 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Packaging question for our pilot — need options

Alice — thanks for the usage sheet, it helps a lot. Looping Ben and Marissa for pricing + contracting input. Below are three pragmatic paths we often recommend, with pros/cons tailored to your bursty profile.

1) Short reserved tier (pilot reservation) — recommended if you want predictable latency and can tolerate a short commitment.
   - 3-month pilot: reserve a small Dedicated pool equivalent to ~0.5 GPU sustained (we price as one shared slot).
   - Flat pilot fee: $12,000/month (covers baseline throughput, priority routing, and 1x concierge onboarding session).
   - Pros: predictable latency, simplified SLOs during pilot, easier path to full Dedicated conversion.
   - Cons: you'd pay for reserved capacity even in low-use windows; may be overkill if usage stays very low.

2) Consumption pilot (capacity credits) — lower upfront commitment, pay-as-you-go with token credits.
   - Pilot credit pool: pre-buy $18k in capacity credits valid for 3 months (we estimate ~250k tokens/month avg plus burst headroom).
   - Pros: no reservation, cost aligns to actual usage; easier to start.
   - Cons: less predictable latency during large bursts unless we configure burst buffer; harder to convert directly to Dedicated.

3) Blended pilot + concierge (recommended for teams that need operational hand-holding).
   - 3-month blended: $9k/month reserved + $6k capacity credits + Concierge add-on $4k one-time (4 tuning hours + weekly syncs).
   - Includes one amendment template that eases transition to Dedicated within 6 months.
   - Pros: balance of cost and reliability; concierge reduces integration time.
   - Cons: slightly more complex billing; requires sign-off on amendment.

Ben/Marissa — can you confirm margin-friendly price ranges and any notes on standard amendment language (we may need a reduced-term conversion clause and a rollback for credits if they don't convert)?

Next steps for Alice: tell us whether you prefer 1/2/3 and we'll draft the pilot amendment + pricing worksheet.

—Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Packaging question for our pilot — need options

Quick internal note with concrete numbers and contracting guidance. I ran the pilot usage against our unit economics and here are pragmatic commitments we can offer without requiring exec approval: 

- Option A (Reserved pilot): $12k/mo (Mateo's number) OK. We keep it as a 3-month minimum (no auto-renew), include one 60% discount on overage credits for that period, and standard SLOs. Attach amendment stub: starlit_amendment_v2.pdf.

- Option B (Consumption credits): Recommend $18k pre-buy credit but offer 10% bonus credits if they purchase within 5 business days (makes conversion easier). We need to ensure we set a burst buffer of 120k tokens/day at no extra charge for pilot.

- Option C (Blended + Concierge): I like this for Starlit. Revised numbers to preserve margin: $8k/mo reserved + $5.5k credits + Concierge $3.5k one-time (covers 6 tuning hours + kickoff). In exchange we ask for a non-binding rollout intention clause and a 30-day notice window if they want to terminate pilot early (standard).

Contract asks for Legal:
- A simple DocuSign addendum that converts pilot credits to month-to-month consumption at the same unit rate if they don't convert.
- A statement that concierge work is professional services and billed separately (non-refundable if scheduled).

If Mateo and Marissa are good with the blended figures, I'll prepare the amendment + editable pricing worksheet and share the DocuSign link.

Cheers,
Ben
Sr. Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

Attachments: starlit_amendment_v2.pdf (PDF, 48KB)

From: Alice Chen <alice@starlithealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Patrick O'Neill <patrick.oneill@starlithealth.com>, Legal Ops <legal@starlithealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:41:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Packaging question for our pilot — need options

Thanks — this is very helpful and much clearer. We'd like to proceed with Option C (Blended + Concierge) given our integration needs. Two quick asks before we sign:

1) Can you lower the concierge fee to $3k one-time if we sign the pilot amendment within 72 hours?
2) Legal requests the DocuSign link and a copy of the amendment with redlines for our counsel to review (priority items: data retention clause and an escape clause if pilot impacts PHI workflows).

If you can confirm the $3k concierge incentive and send DocuSign, we'll route to Legal and aim to start the pilot in the week of May 17.

Best,
Alice
Principal Product Manager, Starlit Health

--
Alice Chen
Starlit Health

DocuSign request: DocuSign:0000-ABCD
Attachments: none

title: Tri-region deep-dive: rotating round-robin availability + DST gotchas
preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Lina Torres <lina.torres@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Tri-region deep-dive: rotating round-robin availability + DST gotchas

Hi Lina / Mateo,

Thanks for agreeing to a technical deep-dive where our solutions team paired with your architects will walk through integration, performance expectations, and a short run of a sample workload. To make this practical for all three regions (US / EU / APAC) we want to avoid a single static time — proposing a rotating round-robin so each region occasionally gets an evening-friendly session.

Quick context and asks: 
- Goal: 90-minute deep-dive (architecture + Q&A + short hands-on).
- Attendees (tentative): Lina (Acme), Rajiv (NebulaCorp SE) — if Rajiv wants to join pls confirm; Mateo (Redwood SE) + Marissa (Redwood Solutions) + me.
- Constraint: APAC prefers sessions that start >= 18:30 SGT; EU prefers 09:00-17:30 CET; US prefers 08:30-18:00 PT/ET.
- DST gotcha: US DST starts earlier than EU this year — recommend we anchor invites in UTC to avoid conversion errors.

I've attached a quick overlap_matrix_v1.csv showing candidate windows in UTC and local equivalents. Can each of you reply with preferred windows (rank 1-3) from the sheet, and call out any hard blocks?

Suggested initial candidate windows (UTC):
- Option A: 13:00–14:30 UTC (works for EU afternoon, US morning)
- Option B: 18:00–19:30 UTC (works for APAC early morning, EU late afternoon)
- Option C: 21:00–22:30 UTC (APAC evening, US afternoon)

If everyone is OK I'll send a single rotating invitation and mark the series as UTC timestamps. If we need to preserve local-time labels we should include explicit offsets in the body.

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Senior Solutions Manager
Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.com

Attachment: overlap_matrix_v1.csv (CSV: candidate UTC slots + local equivalents)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Lina Torres <lina.torres@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T11:03:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Tri-region deep-dive: rotating round-robin availability + DST gotchas

Kim — thanks for kicking this off. I ran the overlap matrix against typical customer architects' calendars. Quick notes: 
- I can lead the walkthrough; prefer Option C if Acme wants an APAC-friendly time every third session.
- Important: if we schedule by local title (e.g., \"5pm CET\") and the invite repeats across DST boundaries we'll get drift. UTC series avoids the problem.

My ranked availability (from your sheet):
1) Option C (21:00–22:30 UTC) — I can start earlier if needed, will stay for full 90.
2) Option A (13:00–14:30 UTC) — OK but will be tight for US West coast attendees.
3) Option B (18:00–19:30 UTC) — possible but Rajiv earlier said APAC may not be awake for that unless it's their morning.

One other ask: can we keep a rolling note in the invite indicating which region the session is optimized for (e.g., \"APAC-optimized\") so internal ops know to have recording + async notes?

Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com

From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@acme.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-05T07:22:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Tri-region deep-dive: rotating round-robin availability + DST gotchas

Thanks Kimberly — the team at Acme (me + Rajiv) prefer a 90-minute session. Rajiv asked me to confirm he can join; I've copied him on his own calendar thread (we'll introduce him in the invite) — his address is rajiv.singh@nebulacorp.com.

Our ranked preferences: 
- 1: Option B (18:00–19:30 UTC) — hits EU late afternoon and APAC early morning; Rajiv can take it occasionally (he prefers mornings).
- 2: Option C (21:00–22:30 UTC) — OK occasionally but later for EU folks; Rajiv flagged timezone fatigue if done frequently.
- 3: Option A (13:00–14:30 UTC) — not ideal for our APAC architects.

If you do a rotation, can we ensure the first slot is APAC-optimized so their engineers see the hands-on portion live? Also would prefer UTC anchor + explicit local times in invite (we use both).

Lina Torres
Director, Integrations — Acme Systems
lina.torres@acme.com

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina.torres@acme.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-05T15:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Tri-region deep-dive: rotating round-robin availability + DST gotchas

Thanks both — good input. Based on responses I'm proposing we: 

1) Run a rotating weekly/biweekly series (90 minutes) and label each occurrence with a region tag. First three occurrences: APAC-optimized, EU-optimized, US-optimized.

- Constraint: APAC prefers sessions that start >= 18:30 SGT; EU prefers 09:00-17:30 CET; US prefers 08:30-18:00 PT/ET.

title: Re: EmberForge SDK — compatibility with LlamaIndex/LangChain and tracing guidance
preview:
From: Evan Park <evan.park@emberforge.io>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Sana Iqbal <sana.ember@emberforge.io>, Partnerships <partners@emberforge.io>
Date: Sun, 2 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: EmberForge SDK — quick intro + compatibility ask

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for taking a call last week — follow-up from EmberForge. We build a lightweight embedding & prompt-management SDK that a lot of our ISV partners wire into LangChain/LlamaIndex-based workflows. A few items we want to align on with Redwood before we start a pilot: 

- Official guidance on tracing / attribution hooks so Kamin (our runtime) can emit traces to Redwood Console without leaking PII.
- Compatibility matrix for your hosted + dedicated variants (quantized variants, KV cache behavior with embeddings).
- A short SOW template and preferred procurement flow for an initial sandbox test (2 weeks).

Attached: emberforge_compat_matrix_v1.csv (our initial questions) and a draft SOW. Can you please loop in the right technical contacts?

Goal: ship a small sample integration that demonstrates: (1) embed + vector store using your embedding endpoint, (2) instrumented trace events surfaced in Redwood Console, (3) a fallback policy to a lower-cost model.

We’d love to start the pilot the week of May 17 if that schedule makes sense.

Thanks — Evan

--
Evan Park
Head of Partnerships, EmberForge
evan.park@emberforge.io
https://emberforge.io


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Evan Park <evan.park@emberforge.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: EmberForge SDK — quick intro + compatibility ask

Evan — thanks for the clear ask and for the attachments. Quick readthrough and next steps: 

- Tracing: Karthik will own the spec for trace ingestion and redaction guidelines. We support direct trace hooks to Redwood Console (API key + per-tenant sampling) and an agented mode for private deployments.
- Compatibility: Our hosted models have a few quantized builds; the biggest caveat is prefix caching for models that use custom tokenizers — we’ll flag those in the matrix.
- Procurement/SOW: Vanessa can drive contracting and will send a procurement checklist.

We’ll aim to:
1) Return an annotated compat_matrix (in-line comments) by Wed, May 5.
2) Propose a pared-down SOW for a 2-week sandbox by Fri, May 7.
3) Kickoff tech sync the week of May 10 (45 min).

Looping in Marissa (PM for ISV partnerships) and Karthik (runtime/instrumentation) — they’ll intake the tech asks. Is Kamin open to a small sample repo with a tracing shim (MIT license) we can point to? That speeds up validation.

Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partnerships Manager, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Evan Park <evan.park@emberforge.io>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 May 2027 08:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: EmberForge SDK — quick intro + compatibility ask

Evan, thanks for the clear matrix — I left inline notes in the CSV (see attachment). High-level technical notes here to speed the kickoff: 

- Trace schema: Redwood uses a compact span model: {service, trace_id, span_id, event, metadata}. We accept both OTLP and a REST push. For ISV shims we recommend REST for simplicity (API key + dataset header).
- PII redaction: do not send full prompt text in trace metadata unless customer consents. Use prompt hash + tokensize + token_preview (first/last 16 tokens masked). We will provide a client helper that performs the hash+mask.
- KV cache & embeddings: For models with truncated KV caches (long-context quantized builds) you may see higher token re-fetch rates. If your SDK pins session_ids, enable warm_start: true to reuse KV aggressively.
- Sampling & cost controls: set sampling_rate: 0.05 for production-level telemetry; 1.0 for sandbox runs.

Attachments referenced: I uploaded an annotated compat matrix (emberforge_compat_matrix_v1.csv) with three colors: green = good, amber = needs config, red = unsupported.

For the sample repo: MIT-licensed shim is fine. Please include a README that shows: 1) how to switch tracing mode (rest vs otlp) and 2) how to swap embedding endpoints for hosted/dedicated. We can host a short example in our docs.

Karthik

--
Karthik Iyer
Lead, Runtime Instrumentation, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

title: Argonautech: Immersion week — arrival windows, local host & visa runbook
Sprint commit lift, soft-capacity window & rateband clarifications
preview:
From: Carol Nguyen <carol@frescoretail.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procure@frescoretail.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sprint commit lift, soft-capacity window & rateband clarifications

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team was impressed with Redwood's latency and the KV cache benefits. Our Q3 roadmap has a heavier burst pattern (holiday promotions) and we want to avoid throttles during critical campaign windows.

A few asks so we can align procurement and legal this week:
- Can you send a clear pricing delta for a monthly commit uplift from 2M tokens/mo to 3M tokens/mo, including the per-token rate for the elevated tier and the overage rate if we exceed the 3M?
- We'd like a soft-capacity window for up to an additional 30% burst for three weeks per quarter without immediate overage billing — what guardrails and rateband would Redwood recommend?
- Please include any SLA adjustments tied to commit increases (latency SLO, scheduled maintenance windows).

Attached: usage_report_Mar2027.csv and the current SOW (FR_SOW_v1) for reference. Procurement will need a DocuSign-ready SOW and a PO template if we move forward.

Can we get a ballpark quote and a suggested rollout timeline by EOD Wednesday (4/22)? Happy to jump on a quick call if that helps.

Best,
Carol Nguyen
Head of Platform Partnerships
Fresco Retail
---
Attachment: usage_report_Mar2027.csv (CSV)
Attachment: FR_SOW_v1.pdf (SOW stub)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Carol Nguyen <carol@frescoretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 12:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sprint commit lift, soft-capacity window & rateband clarifications

Hi Carol — thanks for the clear note and the report. Short answers up front, longer details below:

1) We can support a firm monthly commit increase from 2M -> 3M tokens.
2) We can provision a quarterly soft-burst allowance up to 30% (technical guardrail + accounting flow) for three consecutive weeks, with a fallback soft-throttle and grace overage accounting.
3) SLA adjustments are possible; we'll call out latency SLO parity with current plan and document maintenance blackout windows for your campaign weeks.

I'll need a quick internal check on exact per-token tiers and whether your account qualifies for the promotional uplift pricing. Karthik / Kimberly — can you confirm the uplift pricing bands and whether a 12-month committed term with annualized billing would unlock a lower unit rate?

Carol, I'll get a formal quote and a redlined SOW (DocuSign-ready) by Wednesday EOD. If procurement wants, we can include a 30-day pilot for the first uplift month to validate burst behavior.

Propose a 30 minute call tomorrow at 10am PT to walk the PO path and timing. Let me know if that works.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: (none)

- Compliance: the SOW must note that logging retention and KV cache eviction policies remain as agreed in the existing contract.

title: Co-engineering bundle proposal & SDK co-maintenance scope
preview:
From: Devina Kumar <devina@coengineer.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Partnerships <partnerships@coengineer.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 14:02:00 -0700
Subject: Co-engineering bundle proposal & SDK co-maintenance scope

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the quick intro last week. CoEngineer is seeing a growing set of customers asking for a packaged SDK that makes it trivial to use Redwood as the hosted runtime while keeping favorite tooling like LangChain and LlamaIndex plug-and-play.

High-level ask: would Redwood consider a co-maintained "SDK bundle" that includes:
- a LangChain connector optimized for Redwood routing & streaming,
- a LlamaIndex node store + retriever prewired for Redwood embeddings and cache semantics, and
- a lightweight tracing / telemetry shim that surfaces Redwood request IDs and token-level latency into OTEL-compatible traces.

We think a co-bundled offering (CoEngineer + Redwood branding options) could accelerate adoption for mutual customers and lower integration support load.

Can we schedule a 30-min sync this week to align on: scope split, repo ownership, initial sprint plan, and licensing? Suggested attendees: your SDK/infra lead and someone from product. I can bring our PM and engineering lead.

Thanks,

Devina Kumar
Head of Product Partnerships
CoEngineer

---
Attachment: coengineer-proposal.pdf (1 page summary)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Devina Kumar <devina@coengineer.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 16:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Co-engineering bundle proposal & SDK co-maintenance scope

Devina — great to hear from you and excited by the idea. 30 minutes sounds good.

Proposed times (PDT):
- Tue 13 Apr, 10:00–10:30
- Wed 14 Apr, 15:00–15:30

Goals for the sync:
- agree a minimal initial surface (LangChain retriever + LlamaIndex node store + tracer shim),
- decide which repo will host the adapters and who maintains CI,
- define an 8-week MVP roadmap with clear handoffs.

Marissa and Karthik — looping you both in to triage engineering capacity and feasibility. If we go ahead, I expect this will touch runtime adapter work (into Redwood serving runtime), a test harness for compatibility, and a lightweight packaging layer for ISV distribution.

I can take the meeting lead and draft a mutual action plan afterward.

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference

---


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: CoEngineer bundle — capacity & proposed split

Quick internal note after reviewing the proposal draft and our current roadmap. High-level thoughts: this is strategically valuable but requires careful scoping to avoid touching our core runtime too much. Proposed split: 

Redwood scope (engineering):
- implement adapter layer in runtime to expose a small public shim (streaming + token-level hooks),
- provide an official embeddings facade for LlamaIndex usages (backwards-compatible),
- add e2e compatibility tests and a CI job template.

CoEngineer scope (partner):
- package LangChain/LlamaIndex wrappers and example apps,
- own distribution packaging, release notes, and enterprise docs,
- first-line customer support for SDK integration issues.

Deliverables & timeline (tentative):
- Week 1–2: design doc + API contract; repo created (redwood/coengineer-sdk-bundle),
- Week 3–6: implementation of adapters + tests,
- Week 7–8: integration validation with CoEngineer example apps and initial docs.

Risks / asks:
- we need a product decision on whether we accept co-branding vs. "supported by Redwood" only,
- add a small SRE budget for the CI template run time.

If this looks reasonable I can convert to a short mutual action plan and share with CoEngineer post-sync. Thoughts?

Marissa Cole
Director, ISV Partnerships (Redwood)


From: Devina Kumar <devina@coengineer.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Partnerships <partnerships@coengineer.com>, engineering@coengineer.com
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:33:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Co-engineering bundle proposal & SDK co-maintenance scope

Thanks Mateo & team — great sync yesterday. Attaching a draft Mutual Action Plan (MAP) with deliverables and acceptance criteria. Highlights:
- CoEngineer to ship LangChain adapters and sample notebook by week 5,
- Redwood to ship runtime shim + CI template by week 6,
- Joint validation window in week 7, followed by documentation handoff.

Attachment: CE-SDK-MAP.xlsx (spreadsheet with milestones & owners)

We're OK with "Supported by Redwood + CoEngineer" branding for the first release; we can revisit co-brand guidelines later.

If everything looks good, I'll send a DocuSign for the partner addendum (simple T&Cs + support SLA) — I included a preview link below.

DocuSign preview: https://www.docusign.com/en/link/example-doc/CE-MAP-2027

Regards,
Devina Kumar
Head of Product Partnerships
CoEngineer

---


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Devina Kumar <devina@coengineer.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 09:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Co-engineering bundle proposal & SDK co-maintenance scope

Devina — thanks for the MAP. We reviewed and are good to proceed with the proposed split and timeline. Two quick items before you send the DocuSign:

1) Can you add a single line that clarifies ownership of telemetry traces (we will export Redwood request IDs to OTEL; CoEngineer agrees not to re-ingest PII in traces).
2) Please attach the sample license text for the SDK bundle (we prefer Apache 2.0 or similar permissive).

Next steps on our side:
- I'll create the GitHub repo and add a skeleton with issue templates: https://github.com/redwood-inference/coengineer-sdk-bundle,
- Marissa will open two internal tickets to reserve engineering cycles and CI budget,
- Karthik will scope the runtime shim and post an RFC in 3 business days.

Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: CE-SDK-MAP.xlsx
Related: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2bC-EG-CoEngineer-Shared

title: Request: short demo to review GitHub integration + self-hosting concerns
preview:
From: Lina Park <lina@openhook.dev>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T16:12:00Z
Subject: Interest in Redwood GitHub integration — quick demo?

Hi Mateo,

We signed up for Redwood yesterday after reading the GitHub integration docs. OpenHook is an open-source project (MIT) with a small core team and a hosted commercial org. We’re evaluating how Redwood would fit into our onboarding flow for repo webhooks and for customers that want to self-host.

Two quick asks:
- Can a short demo cover webhook authentication options (app vs OAuth), permission scopes, and installing on GitHub orgs vs user accounts?
- We also want to understand self-hosted/private deployment constraints (VPC/NAT, webhook routing).

Available next week in PDT mornings — do you have a 30-min slot? If helpful, here’s a repo and a short diagram: https://github.com/openhook/ingress-sample

Thanks —
Lina Park
Engineering Lead, OpenHook
https://openhook.dev

P.S. Ops lead Tom (tom@openhook.dev) will likely join if the time works.


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Lina Park <lina@openhook.dev>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T19:07:00Z
Subject: Re: Interest in Redwood GitHub integration — quick demo?

Hi Lina — great to meet you and thanks for the signup + repo link. I’m Mateo on the SDR team; I’ll kick off the demo and bring a solutions engineer (Ben) for technical depth and Marissa (AE) to cover pricing/roadmap context.

A few quick clarifying Qs so we tailor the session:
- Is OpenHook evaluating Redwood for a hosted integration across your customers, or primarily for internal self-hosted deployments?
- Rough scale: how many repos/orgs would you expect to attach initially (10, 100, 1000)?
- Any specific compliance constraints (SOC2/PII) we should prepare for?

Proposed 30-min slots (PDT):
- Tue 2027-04-20 09:00–09:30
- Tue 2027-04-20 11:00–11:30
- Wed 2027-04-21 09:30–10:00

If one of those works I’ll send a calendar invite and attach a short checklist for the call (permissions we’ll demo). Attached: pre-demo-checklist.txt (stub).

If none work, drop a couple times that work for you and Tom and we’ll adjust.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
SDR, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

Hi Mateo — thanks for the quick response and for looping Ben/Marissa.

Quick update: we have a scheduled release on Tue 4/20 that’s likely to conflict with the morning slots. Would you be able to move to the week after? The two windows that work for us (PDT) are:
- Tue 2027-04-27 10:00–10:30
- Thu 2027-04-29 08:30–09:00

Also, Tom will join (cc’d). For the agenda, we’d love explicit examples of:
- rotating webhook secrets in a self-hosted/private deployment
- route fallback when a customer’s webhook endpoint is unreachable
- install flow for org admins vs individual repos

Thanks — we’re excited to see the flow end-to-end.

From: Lina Park <lina@openhook.dev>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T16:12:00Z
Subject: Interest in Redwood GitHub integration — quick demo?

Hi Mateo,

We signed up for Redwood yesterday after reading the GitHub integration docs. OpenHook is an open-source project (MIT) with a small core team and a hosted commercial org. We’re evaluating how Redwood would fit into our onboarding flow for repo webhooks and for customers that want to self-host.

Two quick asks:
- Can a short demo cover webhook authentication options (app vs OAuth), permission scopes, and installing on GitHub orgs vs user accounts?
- We also want to understand self-hosted/private deployment constraints (VPC/NAT, webhook routing).

Available next week in PDT mornings — do you have a 30-min slot? If helpful, here’s a repo and a short diagram: https://github.com/openhook/ingress-sample

Thanks —
Lina Park
Engineering Lead, OpenHook
https://openhook.dev

P.S. Ops lead Tom (tom@openhook.dev) will likely join if the time works.

Hi Lina — great to meet you and thanks for the signup + repo link. I’m Mateo on the SDR team; I’ll kick off the demo and bring a solutions engineer (Ben) for technical depth and Marissa (AE) to cover pricing/roadmap context.

A few quick clarifying Qs so we tailor the session:
- Is OpenHook evaluating Redwood for a hosted integration across your customers, or primarily for internal self-hosted deployments?
- Rough scale: how many repos/orgs would you expect to attach initially (10, 100, 1000)?
- Any specific compliance constraints (SOC2/PII) we should prepare for?

Proposed 30-min slots (PDT):
- Tue 2027-04-20 09:00–09:30
- Tue 2027-04-20 11:00–11:30
- Wed 2027-04-21 09:30–10:00

If one of those works I’ll send a calendar invite and attach a short checklist for the call (permissions we’ll demo). Attached: pre-demo-checklist.txt (stub).

If none work, drop a couple times that work for you and Tom and we’ll adjust.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
SDR, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

Subject: Re: Interest in Redwood GitHub integration — quick demo?

Hi Mateo — thanks for the quick response and for looping Ben/Marissa.

Quick update: we have a scheduled release on Tue 4/20 that’s likely to conflict with the morning slots. Would you be able to move to the week after? The two windows that work for us (PDT) are:
- Tue 2027-04-27 10:00–10:30
- Thu 2027-04-29 08:30–09:00

Also, Tom will join (cc’d). For the agenda, we’d love explicit examples of:
- rotating webhook secrets in a self-hosted/private deployment
- route fallback when a customer’s webhook endpoint is unreachable
- install flow for org admins vs individual repos

Thanks — we’re excited to see the flow end-to-end.

Claire — thanks for the roster and MAP draft. Quick read and a proposal below. We can absolutely get you an Exec Briefing before May 2; want to make sure the MAP is laser-focused so there are no last-minute blockers.

High-level proposed MAP checkpoints (Redwood owners in parentheses):
1) Security/compliance checklist signoff (Neha Kapoor) — SOC2 evidence, data retention table, access audit window.
2) Integration readiness (Karthik Iyer) — sample payloads, throughput targets, mock inference runbook.
3) Cost & performance validation (Mateo Alvarez) — expected latencies and a token-cost projection for pilot traffic.
4) Rollout guardrails & rollback plan (Karthik) — canary thresholds and fallback model variants.
5) Executive briefing materials (Mateo & Karthik) — 10m exec summary, 20m demo, 25m Q&A.

Owners + ETA: aim to have 1–3 completed (security signoff, integration readiness, exec materials draft) by 2027-04-26 COB so we can rehearse and ship the final Exec Brief slide pack on 2027-04-27 morning.

Candidate Exec Briefing slots (60m):
- Tue, 27 Apr 2027 10:00-11:00 PT
- Mon, 25 Apr 2027 15:00-16:00 PT

Can you confirm which slot works for you and Raj? Action items for Nimbus to unblock the MAP: provide the final list of attendees who will ask questions on security/contract, and confirm an initial telemetry retention requirement (90/180/365 days).

I'll loop in Marissa for any contract-level asks if we need commercial flexibility.

Mateo Alvarez
Head of Solutions | Redwood Inference

title: Order form scope rewrite — implementation governance & pricing tradeoffs
preview:
From: Hannah Lee <hannah.lee@medlytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Medlytics Procurement <procurement@medlytics.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Order form scope rewrite — proposal and acceptance criteria

Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our engineering and product teams did a deeper review and we'd like to propose a scope rewrite for the current Order Form — effectively moving a set of features into a new Exhibit F that reflects the upcoming Gen-A model tuning work.

High-level asks from our side:
- Can Redwood produce a formal change order that creates Exhibit F (technical acceptance + phased rollout) rather than amending the core MSA?
- We need visibility on the pricing delta for the fork-lifted scope (estimated + expected monthly spend).
- Technical acceptance criteria: we will need a brief test plan and SLA language (latency and throughput baselines) to sign off.
- We're asking for a rollback window (45–60 days) after go-live in case the integration affects downstream pipelines.

Attached: scope-redline-v1.docx (marked-up SOW). Also shared a Drive folder with the team's notes: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-medlytics-scope

Can you loop in pricing and legal? We'd like to target a quick decision — procurement hopes to sign a change order by the middle of next week.

Best,
Hannah
Hannah Lee
Head of Platform Partnerships | Medlytics Inc.
Phone: +1 415-555-0182

---
Attachment: scope-redline-v1.docx (Word doc, 72KB)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Lee <hannah.lee@medlytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order form scope rewrite — proposal and acceptance criteria

Hannah —

Appreciate the clear markup and the drive link. I've looped in Laura (sales ops) and Karthik (legal/deals) to help size the commercial impact and run the redlines. Quick reaction from my side:

Option A (fast top-up): add the new features to the existing Order Form as a consumption-tier top-up. Smaller paperwork (change order), faster to get you live, but pricing will reflect the immediate capacity premium.

Option B (phased Exhibit F): create a separate Exhibit F that formalizes the new SOW and includes technical acceptance phases and a rollback window. This requires more detailed MSA exhibit redlines but gives clearer long-term economics and rollback governance.

From a timelines perspective: if you need sign-off by next Wednesday, Option A is more likely unless we compress legal review. Option B is preferred for both sides if we can agree a 60-day pilot pricing and a short acceptance test plan (we'll provide a 3-step test plan: smoke, perf baseline, end-to-end validation).

I've attached a short note with two commercial templates for consideration (redwood-proposal-options.pdf). Can we set a 30-minute technical alignment call tomorrow to lock acceptance criteria?

Thanks,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0124

---
Attachment: redwood-proposal-options.pdf (PDF, 48KB)

1) Review the attached redwood-proposal-options.pdf and Hannah's scope-redline-v1.docx and identify the minimum set of MSA/exhibit edits required for Option B (phased Exhibit F). Look specifically for: acceptance criteria language, rollback/termination mechanics for the exhibit, and any carve-outs for data residency or KMS usage requested by Medlytics.
2) If Option B requires new security language (e.g., stricter audit log retention or specific KMS/HSM references), flag that immediately — procurement will want a heads-up since their legal team may push back.
3) If we can, provide a redline of the MSA exhibit by COB Tue (Apr 13). If that's not feasible, propose the fastest partial change order language we can run for next-week signature.

I'm happy to join any call with Medlytics legal — Laura, please prepare a short commercial summary of expected delta (T-0 / T+30 / monthly run rate) so procurement has numbers.

Thanks,
Mateo

title: Kinetix: request for productization/ownership carveout — routing & proposed language
preview:
From: Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: legal@kinetix.ai
Date: Fri, 9 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Mateo —

Thanks for the quick demo yesterday. Our legal team reviewed the MSA + order form and pushed a redline seeking an assignment-style carveout for a set of defined product outputs so we can commercialize the derived features in our product. Key asks:

- Explicit assignment (not merely a license) for outputs generated by Kinetix prompts where the model uses customer-provided proprietary training data.
- A narrow list of allowed downstream use cases (productization into Kinetix SaaS only; no resale).
- A short training opt-out window (60 days) where we can request we not be used for model retraining after go-live.

We attached the redline (Kinetix_MSA_redline_v3.docx). Can Redwood confirm whether this is something you can accept, or propose alternate language? If assignment is a non-starter, a long-term exclusive license would be an option.

We'd like to close this before procurement reviews pricing next week.

—Anika
Kinetix Legal


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Apr 2027 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Thanks Anika — looping in Legal (Laura), Procurement/Ops (Priyom) and Engineering (Olga) to assess impact and suggest compromise language. Quick internal notes before we dive in:

- Redwood's standard is to grant customers a broad, perpetual license to outputs but we avoid assignments for IP that could impair model safety, auditability, or third-party model relationships.
- We can be flexible for limited, scoped productization (time-boxed, geography-limited, and purpose-limited) with telemetry/attribution requirements and an evidence handoff process.

Next steps we propose:
1) Laura will draft a counterproposal that preserves Redwood's ability to operate/observe telemetry while offering Kinetix the commercial rights they need.
2) Priyom will confirm any discount/credit asks procurement might need if we accept telemetry retention or additional evidence gating.
3) Olga will confirm whether a 60-day training opt-out is operationally feasible and what the engineering controls look like.

Can you confirm: the \"defined product outputs\" — is it a list of API endpoints, a dataset, or specific artifacts you expect to own? If you can share a short inventory, that will help us scope the carveout.

I'll follow up with a consolidated redline by EOD Monday.

Best,
Mateo
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Anika / team —

We reviewed Kinetix's redline. Assignment of IP is our main red flag — it introduces transfer of rights that can affect our model servicing and third-party license obligations. That said, we can offer a narrowly-tailored compromise. Below is draft language I suggest we propose back (summarized):

- \"Grant\": Redwood grants Kinetix a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, transferrable (to affiliates) license to use, reproduce, modify and distribute Outputs for the sole purpose of integrating Outputs into Kinetix's SaaS offering to its end customers.
- \"Exclusivity\": If Kinetix requires exclusivity for a specific output, Redwood can offer a time-limited exclusive license (e.g., 12 months) in exchange for a higher commitment level / revised pricing.
- \"Training\": Redwood will not use Kinetix-provided proprietary datasets for model retraining for a 60-day opt-out period following written request at go-live; after the opt-out period Redwood may retain telemetry and aggregated usage for product improvement subject to anonymization.
- \"Attribution and Evidence\": For productized outputs Kinetix will receive an evidence package (metadata, prompt fingerprints, and model version) necessary for audit/compliance — subject to Redwood's confidentiality and third-party obligations.

I attached a text file (redwood-proposal-ownership-language.txt) with the exact wording. Priyom — see pricing note below. Olga — see the training/telemetry ask; can you confirm engineering constraints?

--Laura
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Commercial
Redwood Inference


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 12:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Quick operational/procurement check:

- If we agree to the 12-month exclusivity option, commercial should expect a minimum 20-30% uplift on committed spend or an annual platform fee to offset the opportunity cost and telemetry segregation.
- The 60-day training opt-out is fine as long as Kinetix: (a) provides a definitive list of datasets and endpoints to opt-out for, and (b) accepts a one-time onboarding fee for evidence packaging and longer-term retention (we'll absorb some costs but not all).
- The evidence package can be scoped to a per-feature snapshot (metadata + prompt hash + model version). Anything beyond that (raw prompts, full traces) will require redaction and legal clearance.

I attached a short Ops note (ops-impact-notes.pdf) with implementation bullets and estimated SLAs for evidence delivery.

—Priyom
Head of Customer Ops & Procurement
Redwood Inference


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Anika — thanks for your patience and for sharing the redline. Summary / proposal for next steps so we can close before procurement:

1) Redwood counter: we will NOT assign IP. Instead we offer:
   - Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive license for Outputs for Kinetix's productization purposes, with a 12-month exclusivity option available for specific Outputs in exchange for a commercial uplift (proposal: +25% committed spend or a one-time exclusivity fee).
   - 60-day training opt-out for listed datasets/endpoints at go-live; after that period Redwood may use anonymized telemetry for model improvements.
   - Evidence package: prompt fingerprints, model version, timestamped metadata. Raw prompt content beyond hashes requires approval and may incur a higher fee.

2) If Kinetix insists on assignment rather than license, we need to escalate to execs — that route will likely require a legal escrow and significant commercial concessions.

3) Practical asks from Kinetix to proceed: please send a short inventory of the Outputs you want in-scope and confirm whether exclusivity is required and for what duration. If you agree to the license-based compromise we'll circulate a redline and a short commercial term (Priyom will include pricing adjustments).

Can we plan a 30-min call tomorrow to get this agreed and push the order form to procurement? I'll send a Calendly link if that works.

Best,
Mateo
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference

To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Anika / team —

We reviewed Kinetix's redline. Assignment of IP is our main red flag — it introduces transfer of rights that can affect our model servicing and third-party license obligations. That said, we can offer a narrowly-tailored compromise. Below is draft language I suggest we propose back (summarized):

- \"Grant\": Redwood grants Kinetix a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, transferrable (to affiliates) license to use, reproduce, modify and distribute Outputs for the sole purpose of integrating Outputs into Kinetix's SaaS offering to its end customers.
- \"Exclusivity\": If Kinetix requires exclusivity for a specific output, Redwood can offer a time-limited exclusive license (e.g., 12 months) in exchange for a higher commitment level / revised pricing.
- \"Training\": Redwood will not use Kinetix-provided proprietary datasets for model retraining for a 60-day opt-out period following written request at go-live; after the opt-out period Redwood may retain telemetry and aggregated usage for product improvement subject to anonymization.
- \"Attribution and Evidence\": For productized outputs Kinetix will receive an evidence package (metadata, prompt fingerprints, and model version) necessary for audit/compliance — subject to Redwood's confidentiality and third-party obligations.

I attached a text file (redwood-proposal-ownership-language.txt) with the exact wording. Priyom — see pricing note below. Olga — see the training/telemetry ask; can you confirm engineering constraints?

--Laura
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Commercial
Redwood Inference


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 12:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Quick operational/procurement check:

- If we agree to the 12-month exclusivity option, commercial should expect a minimum 20-30% uplift on committed spend or an annual platform fee to offset the opportunity cost and telemetry segregation.
- The 60-day training opt-out is fine as long as Kinetix: (a) provides a definitive list of datasets and endpoints to opt-out for, and (b) accepts a one-time onboarding fee for evidence packaging and longer-term retention (we'll absorb some costs but not all).
- The evidence package can be scoped to a per-feature snapshot (metadata + prompt hash + model version). Anything beyond that (raw prompts, full traces) will require redaction and legal clearance.

I attached a short Ops note (ops-impact-notes.pdf) with implementation bullets and estimated SLAs for evidence delivery.

—Priyom
Head of Customer Ops & Procurement
Redwood Inference


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Anika Rao <anika@kinetix.ai>

Subject: Re: Proposed carveout for productized outputs (MSA redline)

Anika — thanks for your patience and for sharing the redline. Summary / proposal for next steps so we can close before procurement:

1) Redwood counter: we will NOT assign IP. Instead we offer:
   - Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive license for Outputs for Kinetix's productization purposes, with a 12-month exclusivity option available for specific Outputs in exchange for a commercial uplift (proposal: +25% committed spend or a one-time exclusivity fee).
   - 60-day training opt-out for listed datasets/endpoints at go-live; after that period Redwood may use anonymized telemetry for model improvements.
   - Evidence package: prompt fingerprints, model version, timestamped metadata. Raw prompt content beyond hashes requires approval and may incur a higher fee.

2) If Kinetix insists on assignment rather than license, we need to escalate to execs — that route will likely require a legal escrow and significant commercial concessions.

3) Practical asks from Kinetix to proceed: please send a short inventory of the Outputs you want in-scope and confirm whether exclusivity is required and for what duration. If you agree to the license-based compromise we'll circulate a redline and a short commercial term (Priyom will include pricing adjustments).

Can we plan a 30-min call tomorrow to get this agreed and push the order form to procurement? I'll send a Calendly link if that works.

Best,
Mateo

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo earlier this week — team was impressed. We’re trying to move quickly on a 6‑week pilot with Redwood for the recommendation API, but our finance team (and a large integrator customer) require us to procure quickly on our side, and they don’t have an internal PO ready before the pilot start.

Quick ask: can Redwood onboard LumenAI for a short pilot on an invoice‑only (no PO) basis with these constraints?
- Pilot cap: $5,000 total
- Net30 invoices (electronic invoice)
- Automatic offboarding/termination at pilot end unless PO/MSA is in place
- DPA and basic security questionnaire completed (SOC 2 Type II evidence preferred)

I’ve attached our completed onboarding form and a short SOW so you can see scope (attachments: LumenAI_Onboarding_Form.pdf, SOW_Q1_Short.pdf). If a prepayment or card capture is required for the first invoice to mitigate risk, let me know and I can route that internally — we prefer invoice route but can be flexible.

Could you outline what Redwood needs to approve an exception and an estimated timeline? Happy to hop on a 20 minute call if that helps.

Elena Rossi
Head of Partnerships, LumenAI
elena@lumenai.io

title: Quantify tokens/$ assumptions for MAP — unit economics gating
preview:
From: Sofia Ramos <sofia.ramos@nexahealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@nexahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP ask — need tokens/$ assumptions and gating checklist

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Procurement asked me to capture the MAP gating items that will affect our cost review for the pilot. Specifically they need a clear set of assumptions you are using to translate tokens into dollars for a 30-day pilot and the gating checklist items (what we must validate during eval before procurement final sign-off).

What we want from you by EOD Wednesday:
- One-page tab that shows tokens/day -> $/month for the pilot (we expect roughly 200k tokens/day across chat + summarize flows)
- Explicit assumptions: model variant, tokens per turn, average response length, batching latency tradeoffs, KV/prefix cache hit rates and how they change unit cost, quantization impact (if any)
- Any ops limits that would change price (peak RPS, concurrency, required dedicated capacity)
- A short gating checklist: e.g., 95th pct latency below X ms, cost per 1M tokens under $Y, cache hit rate > Z

Also include a spreadsheet we can hand to procurement. If you need product or CS to join the call to review assumptions, Jordan and I can schedule.

Thanks — this is blocker for our legal and budget review.

Best,
Sofia Ramos
Head of Integrations, NexaHealth
sofia.ramos@nexahealth.com
+1 415-555-0192


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Ramos <sofia.ramos@nexahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@nexahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP ask — need tokens/$ assumptions and gating checklist

Sofia — thanks, attaching a first-pass spreadsheet (nexa_unit_econ_v1.xlsx) and an infra diagram stub. Below are the baseline assumptions I used; I tried to be conservative on caching and optimistic on batching so procurement can see both tails.

Summary (pilot = 30 days, 200k tokens/day average)
- Model: rw-large-v1 (8-bit quantized profile in hosted mode).
- Nominal hosted list-price token rate used for modelling: $0.003 per 1k input+output tokens (base).
- Effective tokens per interaction: 450 tokens (avg: 60 prompt + 390 response).
- Daily tokens: 200k => monthly tokens ~ 6M.

Key modifiers and how they affect $/1M tokens:
- Batching (continuous batching):
  - No batching (worst-case): +25% compute overhead => effective price multiplier 1.25.
  - Conservative batching (small groups, latency-first): price multiplier 1.05.
  - Aggressive batching (throughput-first, up to 100 ms added p90 latency): price multiplier 0.85.
- KV/prefix cache hit rate (applies to prompt tokens saved):
  - Cache hit = 40% (conservative) => ~10% reduction in total tokens served.
  - Cache hit = 65% (realistic for stable workflows) => ~18% reduction.
- Quantization and memory profile: 8-bit quantized runner assumed; negligible incremental cost but enables higher density (affects capacity planning, not per-token price).
- Dedicated capacity surcharge: +12% unit cost for reserved GPU pools (we model both hosted and dedicated rows in the sheet).

Example rows from the attached sheet (rounded):
- Base hosted (no batching, no cache): $18.00 / 1M tokens
- Hosted (conservative batching 1.05, cache 40%): ~$14.6 / 1M tokens
- Hosted (aggressive batching 0.85, cache 65%): ~$9.8 / 1M tokens
- Dedicated (same workload, includes +12% surcharge): add ~12% to the hosted numbers above.

Gating checklist items (MAP gating recommendations)
- Cost gate: Confirm cost under negotiated threshold for pilot \"cost per 1M tokens < $12\" (we recommend using the conservative batching + 40% cache row).
- Latency gate: 95th% latency for interactive flows < 350 ms in your region (can be tightened for high-SLA screens).
- Quality gate: identical eval prompts against rw-large-v1 and your golden set show no regressions > 2% on top-level intent accuracy.
- Ops gate: Peak RPS capacity validated at 2x expected concurrency (we'll run load tests with your traffic profile).

Deliverables attached: spreadsheet with token -> $ math, sensitivity table for cache hit rates and batching, infra diagram (PNG stub). I also pushed a live sheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1NexaUnitEcon

Next steps:
- If this looks good I can add a third column for \"procurement-negotiated price\" assuming a 15% discount off list.
- Recommend a short sync Mon/Tue next week to walk procurement through the sensitivity table.

Attachments: nexa_unit_econ_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood_infra_diagram.png (PNG diagram)

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ramos <sofia.ramos@nexahealth.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Ellis <j.ellis@nexahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 13:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP ask — need tokens/$ assumptions and gating checklist

Looping in a couple of quick asks from sales/procurement perspective — Mateo, solid work on the sensitivity matrix. A few adjustments before we hand to legal/procurement: 

- Please add one \"peak month\" row assuming 3x daily tokens for bursty events (gives procurement worst-case burn).
- Add a line for expected embedding usage (we've seen customers add embeddings for retrieval during pilot; 1M embedding tokens changes cost profile).
- For the cost gate, change the suggested threshold to an absolute pilot budget number as well (e.g., \"Pilot not to exceed $X total\") — procurement likes absolute ceilings.
- Add a quick note on fallback model (if rw-large-v1 not available) — list the compatible smaller model and delta in $/1M tokens.

If we can get those in the sheet I will fold them into the MAP and update the HubSpot deal notes (DEAL-8921). I also want to capture an operational checklist item to validate cache warming during ramp (that usually explains big deltas between lab and real-world caches).

Short reply from Sofia would be great confirming procurement timeline.

—Rishi
Director, Enterprise Sales

Hi Elena —

Great to meet you last week. Per our conversation, I put together a lightweight Mutual Action Plan (MAP) seed and a 3-slide kickoff deck to accelerate a technical eval and decision window for OrbitAI. The goal: get a production-feasible pilot running quickly and capture the acceptance criteria so both teams have a single source of truth.
